likes: It’s easy to use, and uploading documents is fast. I also like the integration with other products, and the pricing feels fair. The technical support is great, and I’m able to stay in contact with other users who can share tips. The AI summary feature is helpful as well.
dislikes: At first, it seemed like a cumbersome product. However, after trial and error and assistance, it was easier to use. Some of the summaries I tried to pull were troublesome. It likes to automatically categorize charges, and it can add more time when reconciling the books. Like a cash app transfer is considered an ATM withdrawal or a wire transfer listed as a DRAW. So we are working through the mislabeling of the transactions.
use_case: I use it for daily reconciliation of transactions. It gives me a clear summary of invoices submitted, payments issued, and it also helps me spot transaction patterns. Easy to create charts of expenditures (good or bad), detailed summaries of the accounts as individuals or altogether.

bonus_data.title: An accounting tool that covers all of your clients' needs

likes:
I am an accountant and a partner with QuickBooks as an advisor to access my clients who use Quickbooks.

I have been doing this for over 10 years and am well acquainted with how QuickBooks works and am very satisfied with it as it makes my life and my clients' lives easier as clients are using a good accounting software to help run thir buiness and gives them the necessary business reports they need.

The cost is lower than most other accounting software for business and can be scaled for more features only when the business needs them.
dislikes: There are not many downsides. But if there is any, I would say the menu layout is not as intuitive or good as some other accounting software solutions
use_case:
QuickBooks Online automates work, such as adding bank feeds for bnk transaction coding, which can then be automated ith rules for recurring types of transactions.
It handles payroll well and payments of employees superannuation payments with ease Affordable, Scalable Accounting Software That Grows with

likes: I like the bank feed download feature in QuickBooks Online because it pulls all of the transactions from the connected bank account. This feature allows me to quickly match transactions to expenses or income, and I can create rules for faster processing. It's great that I don't have to enter each transaction manually since the bank feed pulls directly from the bank and loads the data into the feed, making it easy to categorize or match transactions.
dislikes: I don't like the Integrated Apps change. It used to be easier to download transactions from PayPal to its own bank register. Now it's in a separate area in the integrated apps, so you no longer have it in your bank feed screen. It was hard to locate and also match transactions.
use_case: I use QuickBooks Online for data entry, account reconciliation, and payroll. It allows me to download banking data quickly, process payroll and tax payments, and the bank feed helps categorize and match transactions without manual entry. Efficient Bank Feeds, Needs Integrated Apps

likes:
We’ve had a great experience using QuickBooks. The UI is clean and intuitive, making day-to-day accounting tasks easy to manage, while the integrations with other tools streamline our workflows significantly. Performance has been reliable, with real-time access to financial data across the team.

From a cost perspective, it delivers strong ROI by saving time and improving accuracy. Onboarding was smooth, and the available support resources made it easy to get up and running quickly. Overall, QuickBooks has been a dependable and efficient solution for our financial operations.
dislikes: While QuickBooks Online is a strong overall platform, there are a few drawbacks. Some advanced features can feel less intuitive and require extra time to navigate, and occasional system lag can impact efficiency during heavier use. Pricing can also add up as additional features or users are needed. That said, these are relatively minor compared to the overall value it provides.
use_case: QuickBooks Online helps us streamline core accounting processes like invoicing, expense tracking, and financial reporting, significantly reducing manual work and improving accuracy. Its integrations keep our systems connected, giving us real-time visibility into performance. The built-in AI and intelligent automation features—such as transaction categorization and insights—save time and support better, faster decision-making. Overall, it has improved efficiency, control, and financial clarity across our operations. Clean, Intuitive UI and Reliable Performance That

likes: What I most like about QuickBooks is how easy it is to integrate the OFX and CSV from the bank account. I can see all the bills, I can update the invoices, and check the preview for the month. It is absolutely the best software for financing! The UI is even more great now, a clean design, the performance is smooth, the support has always been great, and pricing is helping us to check the monthly costs and upgrade our services!
dislikes: I wish more people on the team could undo reconciled statements. Sometimes we run into an error in a statement, and the accountant didn’t know it could be undone.
use_case: QuickBooks is solving my problem of forgetting to pay things on time. It helps me keep track of all the bills I need to pay on their exact due dates, avoid missing any bank transactions, and review a monthly overview based on my invoices. QuickBooks Makes Bank Imports and Monthly

likes:
It is the best under-budget, user-friendly accounting software, so even non-accountants can manage basic accounting without much difficulty.

Its integration with other software, like Bill.com, Fishbowl, etc., makes it a complete software package to cover overall performance

A bank account can be synced directly with QBO, so that live bank transactions can be tracked and categorized in the books easily to make it time-efficient and accurate.

As a cloud-based platform, it allows users to access financial data anytime and from anywhere, eliminating the need for installation and enabling seamless collaboration. The QB support system makes it the best in the market.
dislikes: Subscription costs (especially for QuickBooks Online) can feel high for small-scale businesses.
use_case:
Previously, we needed a separate payroll software integrated with QuickBooks to handle payroll. Now, QuickBooks Online’s built-in payroll feature manages everything end-to-end, including processing payroll and filing periodic payroll tax returns.


QuickBooks also solves the problem of manual, error-prone, and scattered financial management by automating accounting and centralizing all financial data, saving time, improving accuracy, and providing real-time insights for better decision-making.

bonus_data.title: One stop solution for complete bookkeeping

likes: What I like best about QuickBooks Online is how easy it is to manage day-to-day accounting in one place. It simplifies invoicing, expense tracking, and bank reconciliations, and the real-time reporting helps keep a clear view of finances. It’s especially useful for collaborating with clients since everything is cloud-based and accessible anytime.
dislikes: What I dislike about QuickBooks Online is that it can feel limiting for more complex accounting needs. Reporting customization isn’t very flexible, and performance can slow down with large data sets. Also, pricing tends to increase over time, and customer support isn’t always consistent.
use_case:
QuickBooks Online solves the problem of managing finances in one place like tracking income, expenses, invoicing, and bank reconciliations.
This benefits me by saving time, reducing manual errors, and giving real-time visibility into financials. It also makes collaboration with clients easier since everything is cloud-based and accessible anytime.

bonus_data.title: Makes managing accounts feel a lot less stressful

likes: I find QuickBooks Online to be a very good and easy app that saves a lot of my time. It simplifies invoice creation so I can make and send them in just a few clicks, which speeds up my work. I also like how it helps me track my expenses without sorting through receipts manually, as it automatically records everything. It keeps all my payments and transactions in one place, making it faster to work and view my finances easily and clearly. It's particularly useful during tax season, keeping all my documents ready without headaches. Additionally, I use it with PayPal, email, payroll tools, Excel, and banking apps, which makes managing everything convenient.
dislikes: Honestly, everything about QuickBooks Online is best. However, it is little difficult to learn the system but we get used to it.

likes: I like how you can quickly search for any customer information. You can create a statement with a balance forward or open item as well. I also like the estimate feature. And I like how it also can automatically chase clients when they haven't paid within their set time frame. It significantly reduces the time that I have to spend chasing clients for payments and searching for a specific invoice or customer engagement item, making everything quick and easy for both myself and the client.
dislikes: Sometimes, the payment link doesn't seem to work properly, and they have to keep refreshing it, and then try again the next week to pay via card. Also, it would be good to have the option to split an invoice and send 50% to one client, 50% to another client.
use_case:
QuickBooks Online simplifies invoicing and payment options, and it reduces the time I spend chasing payments or searching for customer information. Overall, it makes these tasks easier for both me and my clients.
I use Quickbooks Monday-Friday so am very please with the ease of use and number of features i can implement to make my job easier. 
The QuickBooks customer support is normally very quick and efficient. Effortlessly Streamlines Invoicing and

The real-time reporting feature also helps in making quick and informed business decisions.

use_case:
QuickBooks Online solves the problem of managing financial records manually and reduces the risk of errors in accounting. It automates tasks like invoicing, expense tracking, bank reconciliation, and financial reporting, which saves time and improves accuracy.

likes: It’s easy to have transactions automatically pulled in from our bank and cards, and then categorized for accounting. On top of that, I’ve benefited from the tutorials and other resources available, which have been genuinely helpful.
dislikes: Some of the AI features or features that are designed to make things more efficient actually ends up making things less efficient for us because it will categorize incorrectly or make suggestions that are not correct.
use_case: We are a small business with many transactions so it would be very easy for our book keeping methods to be time consuming and not effective. But Quickbooks Online helps us keep everything organized and in one place. Easy Automated Transaction Imports with

likes:
We’ve been using QuickBooks Online across multiple clients and entities, mainly for bookkeeping, payroll tracking, and monthly reporting. What works well is how easy it is to set up and get started, especially for standard accounting workflows.

The bank feeds and rules help save time on repetitive entries, and it integrates with a lot of third-party apps which is useful in our case since every client has a slightly different setup.
dislikes:
One thing that can be frustrating is how it handles multi-entity or non-US setups. We’ve had situations where we needed to configure entities outside the US, and the system doesn’t always adapt smoothly.

Also, in non-US setups, the number of apps supported through the integration feature is very limited. For example, one of our clients in the UK is using Ramp, but QBO UK doesn’t support integration with Ramp, which increases manual work on our end.

On top of that, the user interface changes far too quickly. It keeps getting updated, and it becomes hard to stay familiar with the workflow when the interface is changing so often.
use_case:
It helps us standardize bookkeeping across clients, so we’re not reinventing the process every time. Reporting, reconciliations, and maintaining audit trails feel much more structured than doing things manually.

That said, the reports generated by QBO are quite good. We can actually use them to provide meaningful insights to clients—for example, AR/AP ageing reports, transaction lists by vendor, and similar views that are easy to share and act on. Easy, Flexible Accounting with Seamless

likes: I appreciate the full range of options; QBO really has a monopoly in the online accounting software space. There isn’t much that truly competes with it, largely because of its name recognition. I know that if we use QBO, any accounting professional will be able to work with it. It covers all of our needs.
dislikes: I feel like the reporting dashboards are never 100% accurate or fully trustworthy. The way the information is laid out, along with the recent updates, felt unnecessary and made things harder to follow. Overall, it isn’t very user-friendly unless you’ve had training or have been using it for a long time. Customer support is lacking.
use_case: In my view, every business and organization needs a reliable accounting platform that offers automation and integrations, and is widely recognized by accounting professionals. That’s exactly the need it solves for us. I honestly don’t see anything else on the market that truly competes with it. Comprehensive Accounting, But Reporting and

switched_from.reason: The company forced us. Online is more complicated with many unnecessary features.

likes: QuickBooks Online has been an easy way for our fully remote organization to all have access to the financial data for the organization - in real time. There are new features constantly being rolled out, to better support the organization. It is relatively low-cost, is easy to use, and was relatively easy to set up. There are great integrations to further productivity. We use it daily to weekly. We've had great experience with their customer service as well.
dislikes: Sometimes QuickBooks Online has issues with the file uploads for the various transactions. Additionally, if you are uploading backup files for each transaction, it gets really time consuming. It would be much better if there were a drag/drop function as it would greatly decrease the time taken each month to handle that portion of the financial work.
use_case: QuickBooks Online allows all of our fully-remote staff with financial tasks to easily access the financial data in real-time. We have team members in different parts of the USA that access our financial data, and this allows for us to all work on the finances and see the same information. It is a lower cost software product that allows us to keep up with our financial work easily and allows for easy access for our annual Financial Audits as it allows us to provide access to our CPA that does our Audit. Easy, Low-Cost Real-Time Access to Financials with Great

After that, they started doing direct deposits on every payment and charged us for each deposit. Also, when our customer paid an invoice and then stopped payment, Quickbooks refunded them and charged us without even asking what the situation was, so we ended up having to bill that customer because of the charge they passed on to us.
use_case: Being able to give our accountant access has been really helpful, and it means I don’t have to do as much prep work when tax time comes around. Easy Accountant Access for Fast, Reliable Tax

dislikes: Probably the filing of 1099. Last time we used QuickBooks Online for 1099 filing, it was rejected by the IFRS but successfully processed by QuickBooks Online. We don't know the reason why it was rejected as it didn't indicate in the QuickBooks Online file report. We filed it again a second time, and it was still rejected for unknown reasons, even though all the details of the vendors were correct.

dislikes:
QuickBooks Online has deliberately removed the ability to print IRS-compliant 1099 forms with boxes, a feature that existed for years and is still required for many established workflows.

There is no technical limitation here — the data exists, but the form layout is intentionally suppressed to push users into paid e-filing. This forces businesses and accounting firms to either pay extra, manually re-enter data into IRS PDFs, or use third-party services.

This is a clear regression, not an improvement. It increases cost, wastes time, introduces error risk, and creates unnecessary compliance confusion. Removing core functionality to coerce add-on purchases undermines trust in the platform.

If you rely on predictable, standards-based accounting workflows, be aware that QuickBooks can and will remove them without notice.

likes:
QuickBooks Online has earned its reputation as the industry standard for small business bookkeeping, and it’s easy to see why. The cloud-based platform allows me to access my books from anywhere, whether I’m at my desk or traveling, which is a significant advantage over traditional desktop software. 

Another notable benefit is how smoothly QBO integrates with other Intuit products. The entire ecosystem feels unified—bookkeeping, tax preparation, invoicing, and advisory tools all work together effortlessly. This level of integration streamlines my workflow and helps keep my financial records accurate and consistent across different platforms. For maintaining compliance and building client trust, that reliability is truly invaluable.
dislikes: While QBO offers robust features, it can become costly compared to other options, particularly when you begin adding extra modules. The interface, although effective, may seem daunting to newcomers, and there is a noticeable learning curve for advanced functionalities. Although I have found alternatives that are simpler to use or tailor to my business, what draws me to QBO is the comprehensive Intuit ecosystem, which includes access to live payroll and bookkeeping support whenever I need it.
use_case:
QBO addresses the challenge of maintaining accurate and professional bookkeeping records by offering an industry-standard platform that small businesses trust. Its cloud-based nature means I am no longer restricted to a single computer or office; I can access and update my books from anywhere, which greatly enhances my flexibility. The seamless integration with other Intuit products, such as ProConnect and QBOA, helps eliminate fragmented workflows, making my processes much more efficient. QBO also ensures that my financial records are audit-ready and widely accepted across various industries, which reduces risk and boosts my credibility. The intuitive cloud interface allows me to easily review balances, reconcile accounts, and monitor cash flow without the hassle of searching through paper files or outdated desktop software.

These features benefit me in several ways. I save significant time because bookkeeping, tax preparation, and invoicing are all connected within a single ecosystem. The flexibility to manage my books on the go supports both client responsiveness and business continuity. Integrated workflows help minimize errors and keep my records consistent, which is essential for compliance and fiduciary responsibilities. Using a recognized industry standard also reassures my clients and partners that my records are reliable and well-maintained. Ultimately, by removing technical obstacles, QBO allows me to focus more on providing advisory services and strategic planning, rather than getting bogged down in bookkeeping logistics. Effortless Bookkeeping and Seamless Integration with

likes:
QBO is an industry standard, and because of that, the benefits are numerous. The most obvious are that it’s easy to find employees with QBO experience, there are plenty of software services and add-ons available, most accountants are familiar with it, and the software feels polished and works reliably. Businesses with fewer than 50 employees and roughly $10M to $20M in revenue should have a hard time outgrowing QBO. The reporting options are extensive, and reports are easy to create, customize, and modify. Data entry is also streamlined and efficient. There are advanced options that very small businesses (under 10 employees) likely won’t need, and may not even understand, so there is room for growth.

Implementation is relatively simple, since the layout has been refined over many years. This is essentially an online version of the desktop software they have refined for decades. Integration with other items in your business, like card readers, barcode scanners, etc is relatively simple and choices are numerous.

You will use your accounting software daily. This is an important decision.
dislikes:
I believe that, because they’re an industry standard, they try (and most often succeed) to nickel-and-dime users for all types of services that, in my opinion, are highly overpriced. Yes, you can use their payroll services, inventory services, barcode integrations, and many other add-ons, but the prices are outrageous compared to non-QBO options.

The things I dislike most—and the main reasons I stopped advising my clients to switch to QBO—are essentially twofold.

First, the subscription price has skyrocketed over the last 5–10 years. The basic QBO plan is so limited that even very small businesses (2 employees, <$1M in yearly revenue) will quickly outgrow it and end up needing one of the much more expensive subscriptions. On top of that, the subscription pricing for additional services is also much, much higher than competitors. At the end of the day, you can easily pay twice as much (or more) than what competitors charge for equivalent or even superior products.

Second, the customer service and support department has suffered tremendously over the last 5–10 years; perhaps in an effort to increase shareholder value or profit margins. Getting customer support now is a giant hassle—and it’s expensive, too.

likes: QuickBooks Online has been a game changer for managing my business finances. I love how easy it is to track income and expenses, send invoices, and see real-time cash flow all in one place. The automatic bank feed saves me hours of data entry, and the reporting tools make tax time much less stressful. Since it’s cloud-based, I can access my books anytime, anywhere, which gives me peace of mind and keeps me organized. It’s user-friendly, efficient, and truly helps me stay on top of my business.
dislikes: One downside of QuickBooks Online is the cost, since the monthly subscription and add-ons like payroll can add up quickly. It can also take some time to learn the more advanced features, and customer support isn’t always as fast or helpful as it should be. Occasionally, the bank feeds or connections can glitch, which can be frustrating when trying to stay on top of finances.

Overall, it gives me peace of mind and helps me make smarter financial decisions for my business.

likes: I like most about QuickBooks Online is how convenient and reliable it is for everyday accounting. The bank feed feature stands out the most—it automatically pulls in transactions and makes categorizing expenses much faster. I also appreciate being able to access everything from anywhere, whether I’m in the office or working remotely. The financial reports are easy to understand and actually useful, which helps me make better decisions without needing to be an accounting expert. QuickBooks Online keeps my finances organized and gives me a clear picture of cash flow and overall business performance in one place. It helps avoid last-minute stress during tax season since everything is already up to date. QuickBooks Online saves time by automating things like transaction categorization and bank reconciliations, reduces errors, and makes it much easier to stay on top of cash flow, taxes, and reporting. The switch to QuickBooks Online helped streamline our processes and gave us better visibility into our finances. The initial setup of QuickBooks Online was fairly easy. Connecting bank feeds, and setting up basic company information didn’t take much time. Once everything was set up, day-to-day use became pretty straightforward.
dislikes: While QuickBooks Online is very helpful overall, there are a few things that could be improved. The pricing can feel a bit high, especially as your business grows and you need more features. Some functions are also harder to find than they should be, and occasional updates change workflows, which takes time to adjust to. Additionally, customer support can be hit or miss at times, depending on the issue. Overall it works well, but there’s definitely room for improvement in usability and support consistency.

likes: What I like best about QuickBooks Online is its user-friendly and well-organized interface. The way everything is structured makes registering transactions simple yet very effective. From connecting bank accounts to seamlessly generating invoices and bills, QuickBooks Online provides a complete and efficient solution that helps streamline accounting and bookkeeping processes.
dislikes: While QuickBooks Online is very effective overall, there are a few areas that could be improved. At times, the system can feel a bit slow or laggy when loading certain reports or handling large amounts of data. Some of the more advanced features are also not as intuitive as the core functions, which means it can take extra time to learn or navigate them. Additionally, the subscription costs can add up, especially when needing access to multiple features or users.

likes: What I like best about QuickBooks Online is the real-time access to financial data and the ability to manage books from anywhere. The intuitive dashboard, automated bank feeds, and easy-to-generate reports make it simple to stay on top of cash flow, track expenses, and prepare for tax season. It’s also incredibly helpful that multiple users can collaborate at once without being tied to a specific device or location.
dislikes: One thing I dislike about QuickBooks Online is that some of the more advanced features—like custom reporting or job costing—can feel limited compared to the desktop version or require higher-tier subscriptions. Additionally, the navigation can be a bit clunky when managing multiple properties or accounts, and certain processes (like reconciling complex transactions) sometimes take more steps than necessary. A bit more customization and flexibility would make a big difference.
use_case:
QuickBooks Online solves several key problems by centralizing financial management and simplifying bookkeeping tasks. For property management, it helps us track income and expenses across multiple properties, manage vendor payments, and generate clear financial reports for our clients.

It reduces manual entry through automated bank feeds and recurring transactions, which minimizes errors and saves valuable time. Having cloud access means we can review financials, reconcile accounts, or send invoices from anywhere—which is especially helpful when we’re out in the field or working remotely.

The biggest benefit is the increased financial visibility and organization. It helps us stay compliant, prepare accurate owner statements, and make smarter decisions faster.

We use Quickbooks online for all of our entities. The best parts of using it for us are:
- Ease of use: Quickbooks is intuitive and simplifies navigation for users with minimal accounting experience. 
- Value for money/cost: The pricing is very affordable for us as a company
- Automation, Tax and Reporting: Automated bookkeeping and tax calculations, make it easy to report our compliance and tax reporting, saving us hours.
- Invoicing: We use Quickbooks for all of our invoices, drafting, creating and sending professional invoices. This process is seamless for us with templates, automated reminders, and online payment integration make the process efficient for our team.
- Accountant zccess: Quickbooks allows easy collaboration with our accountant and external advisors to review books, make adjustments, and provide real-time feedback, streamlining tax preparation.
- Industry Acceptance: As a widely adopted platform, Quickbooks is a standard in many industries. New staff typically require little to no training, as familiarity with the software is common, saving time and resources.
- Integration with our other systems: QuickBooks integrates smoothly with our banking systems, and tools like Amex, credit card processors, and expense platforms such as Expensify. This saves us hours of manual processing each week.
dislikes:
We love using Quickbooks online, however the only downside for us is:
- Consolidations: Handling consolidations for businesses with multiple entities or subsidiaries is cumbersome. The process often requires manual workarounds or heavy use of excel.

likes: My organization went with QuickBooks Online because we’re a mid-sized company and needed something that could give us real-time visibility into our financials. What I really like is how easy it is to use from anywhere—the cloud-based setup lets our HR and finance teams work together without worrying about version control. The dashboard gives a good overview of everything from payroll to expenses and outstanding invoices. I also find the bank feed connection and automation for recurring transactions super helpful—it saves us a lot of time on the manual stuff.
dislikes: What I don’t love about QuickBooks Online is that the reporting features—especially for financials and payroll—feel a bit limited. It would be great if we could filter reports by multiple fields at once, like department and project, without having to export everything to Excel. Also, as someone working in Canada, I’ve noticed the payroll features aren’t as flexible as the U.S. version. Things like making T4 adjustments or formatting ROEs could definitely be improved. It works, but there’s room for better support for Canadian businesses.
use_case: QuickBooks Online helps my department manage accounts payable, employee reimbursements, and payroll processing efficiently. For example, when we have to reimburse staff for travel or training expenses, the platform lets us track those reimbursements easily and match them to the correct GL accounts. It also integrates with our payroll tool, so journal entries sync automatically—saving us time during reconciliation at month-end. The integration helps ensure everything stays accurate and aligned with CRA requirements, which is especially important during audits or year-end reporting. QuickBooks

I can easily identify the financial health using the automated graph and cash flow which is the best part of this software.

Also it's send us an notification of the invoices which are overdue and have to pay on urgent basis for saving penalty. Also it is automatically calculate the tax amount which we have to pay at the end of year.

likes: What I like best about QuickBooks Online is that it’s cloud-based, which means I can log in from any computer, anywhere. That flexibility is incredibly convenient. When we first made the switch, it felt familiar enough to the desktop version that the transition was smooth and easy to get behind. It used to be a great fit for our needs.
dislikes: One of the biggest frustrations with QuickBooks Online is the constant updates and forced changes to the interface—it feels like they’re trying to become an all-in-one platform but haven’t quite nailed the execution. The system frequently lags, and I’ve noticed that my login slows down significantly compared to my employees, likely because I’m in it more often. It seems like there’s a time limit or performance drop tied to user activity. Just when you get used to one update, another rolls out, making it hard to keep up. And unfortunately, the customer service hasn’t been helpful enough to make up for these issues.
use_case: Honestly, I’m not sure QuickBooks Online is solving any real problems for us anymore. Many of my clients use it, and a growing number are becoming so frustrated with the constant changes and limitations that they’re considering going back to spreadsheets—and I completely understand why. QuickBooks started as a great solution for small businesses, but it feels like they’ve shifted focus to compete with enterprise-level platforms like Oracle NetSuite. In doing so, they’ve lost sight of the small business users who helped them grow, and unfortunately, those users are now the ones being impacted the most. It's changed so much

likes:
1. User-Friendly Interface:QBO is designed for non-accountants. Its clean, intuitive dashboard makes it easy for small business owners and bookkeepers to manage finances without deep accounting knowledge.

2. Cloud-Based Accessibility: Being fully cloud-based, QuickBooks Online allows you to access your financials anytime, anywhere, from any device with an internet connection. This is great for remote teams and business owners on the go.

 3. Real-Time Collaboration
With QBO, you can invite your accountant, bookkeeper, or team members to access the same system. Multiple users can collaborate in real time, reducing back-and-forth emails and delays.

4. Built-In Invoicing and Payments
You can quickly create branded invoices, send them by email, and even enable online payments via ACH or credit card directly from the invoice—speeding up your receivables.

5. Affordable for Small Businesses
QBO offers tiered pricing plans that are cost-effective for small businesses. You can start with essential features and upgrade as your needs grow.

 6. Automatic Updates and Backups
There’s no need to worry about installing software updates or data backups—QuickBooks Online does this automatically in the background.

7. Basic Inventory, Time Tracking, and Payroll Options
While not as advanced as ERP systems, QBO does offer basic tools for inventory tracking, time tracking, and payroll, all within the same platform.
dislikes:
1. Limited Scalability
QuickBooks Online is ideal for small businesses, but as your company grows (e.g., multiple locations, entities, or international operations), QBO struggles to handle the complexity.
No native multi-entity or consolidated reporting without third-party tools.

2. Basic Reporting and Analytics
While QBO offers standard financial reports, they’re not highly customizable or dynamic. For in-depth KPIs, dashboards, or multidimensional reporting (e.g., by department, location, project), the built-in tools often fall short.

3. Limited Customization
Compared to full ERP systems, QuickBooks Online has limited customization options for forms, workflows, automation, and reporting. Businesses with unique processes may find QBO too rigid.

use_case: QuickBooks Online helped me move away from scattered spreadsheets and manually tracking finances. It’s keeping all my transactions, invoices, and reports organized in one place. This not only saves time but also makes me feel more professional and prepared when sharing financial details with others or reviewing them for myself. It’s definitely boosted my confidence and taught me real-world finance skills. Made Accounting Less Scary for a

4. QuickBooks Online needs secure authentication to ensure added protection.

bonus_data.title: I have ever used for accounting

likes: Inexpensive, user friendly, useful for clients.
dislikes: I do accounting work through Quickbooks for multiple clients, and to be honest, it has been great for the last couple of years. Recently, there was an attempt by a customer to pay using bill pay (Customer using a Credit Card to Pay their invoice). The customers payment did not end up coming through, but I was charged a $486 fee. I was disappointed but understood the fee. 4 months later, I just tried billing another customer for $2,575, the customers card was debited, but I never rec'd the money. It turns out, Quickbooks kept the money. They gave no reasoning and showed it as paid through their software, but it never hit my clients bank account. When I called to ask, they told me they were keeping the money due to the account having fraud in the past (Even thought they understood it was not fraud on our end). The communication has been terrible, this topic goes back 4 months and Quickbooks has not told me not to use bill pay. I can not believe this! I have been on the phone for over 8 hours (90% of the time on hold) talking with mostly friendly representatives, but sadly I have not gotten anywhere. Twice now, I have been on hold for over an hour and the call gets disconnected. I just got an email saying "your bill pay account has been closed" after multiple conversations of representatives trying to figure it out. likes: Quickbooks online is a fantastic tool for accounting firms. Its user-friendly interface makes it easy to navigate, even for those who aren't tech-savy. The ability to access the software from anywhere is incredibly convenient, especially for remote work. The automated features like invoicing, expense tracking, and bank reconciliation save a lot of time and reduce the chance of erros. Additionally, the integration with other financial tools and apps streamlines our workflow, making it more efficient. My company uses Quickbooks Online everyday! Customer support is always there to help when needed and they always get the job done! It's also generally known for its ease of implementation.
dislikes: The learning curve can be steep for new users, especially those unfamiliar with accounting software. Some users might find the customization options limited, which can be a drawback for firms with specific needs. Occasional system glitches which disrupts workflow. Despite these downsides, Quickbooks Online emains a robust and reliable tool for managing accounting tasks efficiently.

use_case:
We used QuickBooks Payments to process ACH transactions for our small business. Unfortunately, three large payments were later reversed after we had already delivered the product, resulting in over $30,000 in losses. These transactions were initially marked as cleared by QuickBooks, and we were given no ability to dispute or intervene in the reversal process.

The loss included $24,747 in unrecoverable physical product that was shipped and later seen resold out of state, which has been reported to the FBI. Despite our efforts to escalate the issue and request assistance, QuickBooks cited their Terms of Service and declined to offer any reimbursement or solution.

This experience has been extremely damaging to our small business. We strongly urge other merchants using QuickBooks to understand that ACH payments processed through this platform are not protected, and can be pulled back months later — even after funds are transferred and fulfillment has occurred.

We’ve since switched to a different processor with stronger fraud tools, delayed fulfillment protocols, and merchant dispute rights. We hope our experience helps other small businesses make more informed decisions. No Protections for

QuickBooks Online has took a prime spot in maintaining our organisation's Accounting and Financial analysis areas.
1. Tax Calculations: Complex tax calculations and compliance with tax liablity law are hectic at the period end. QBO with its feature of segregating tax component from the total invoice/bill amount have made it easier.

2. Financial Analysis: Any business that thrive to make its existence in the market will need a better analysis team that is driven by the tech analysis. Various Reports in QBO covers wide range of areas in the business that may be Human Resource cost, Product Cost, Financial standings of business period after period have been in the important role in analysis. Easy and Convenient mode for Maintaining

likes: QBO is accessible from any of my devices, by any of our users at any time. No installs required. No Single User Mode needed. It has direct connections to our banks and credit cards for easy entry and reconciling.

use_case:
We use QBO for sending invoices, accepting payments online (ACH or CC) or via paper check. 
We pay vendors with QBO bill pay with a few clicks. It allows us to schedule an ACH or Pay by sending a check out without us needing to print, stuff or mail it.

Reconciling the accounts used to be tedious going through bank/cc statements line by line. Now with the bank accounts connected you just go to the banking screen and select a transaction to match it to an existing one, add it if not in already, or even create a rule to do this automatically.

Sales Tax is easier now with the Automatic Calculation of taxes based on location. 

We use integrations with QBO and attach invoices from vendors directly to the transaction. No need to hunt down paperwork, it's literally attached to the transaction in QBO for us. QBO

likes: Only thing is that is available to my bookkeeper online.
dislikes:
I'm reaching out to express my serious frustration with Intuit’s customer service and policies. I need to downgrade my subscription from Advanced to Simple Start—I have no interest in paying $235 per month for a plan that’s beyond what I need.

However, my attempts to make this change have been met with unacceptable roadblocks. The system is designed to push users towards costly options, making it nearly impossible to downgrade without a battle. This kind of tactic, where customers are compelled to jump through hoops just to adjust their plan, is incredibly frustrating and feels exploitative. It’s shocking that I was even asked to pay in advance before a downgrade could be processed!

In addition, the quality of customer support is unacceptable. When I reached out on 11/08/2024 for assistance, I was transferred from one agent to another, none of whom seemed equipped to help. One agent, whom I could barely understand, hung up on me, seemingly at a loss on how to resolve my issue. When I tried using the chat support, I was met with the same lack of competence and assistance, only to be told I would need to wait until Monday since no one was available to help over the weekend.

It’s astonishing that Intuit, a company of this size, would allow a situation like this to unfold, effectively preventing customers from managing their subscriptions easily and efficiently. Forcing customers into upselling conversations when they simply want a downgrade option should not be standard practice—it feels misleading and manipulative. I'm posting this complaint publicly to make others aware and to urge Intuit to reconsider its policies and improve its customer service standards.

likes: The reports and reporting was good and easy to use.
dislikes:
My experience with QuickBooks has been nothing short of a nightmare. I encountered an issue with the payroll setup due to an error on QuickBooks' side, and their support team advised me to cancel the setup. I was explicitly assured by their support representative that canceling the process would not result in any additional credit checks.
However, to my shock and dismay, I discovered multiple hard credit checks on my credit report after following their advice. When I confronted QuickBooks support about this, they informed me that each direct deposit application triggers a credit check and that these checks are irreversible. This is a complete contradiction to the information I was initially given, where I was assured there would be no further checks. Furthermore, I was informed that our payroll threshold should have exempted us from any credit checks in the first place.
The support representatives have proven to be utterly incompetent, providing misleading and incorrect information. Their failure to resolve this issue or even acknowledge their mistake is deeply concerning. It's clear that QuickBooks has no regard for the impact their actions have on their customers' credit ratings and overall financial health.
Despite reaching out multiple times, their response has been dismissive and unhelpful. They merely reiterated their policies without taking any responsibility for the misinformation provided by their own support team. This lack of accountability and transparency is unacceptable for a company handling sensitive financial data.
I strongly advise anyone considering QuickBooks to think twice. Their deceptive practices and inadequate support have caused significant damage to my credit score, and their refusal to rectify the situation speaks volumes about their customer service standards. If QuickBooks does not take immediate and appropriate action to resolve this matter, I will escalate this issue to the credit bureaus, the Better Business Bureau, and any other relevant authorities to ensure they are held accountable for their actions.
Save yourself the trouble and look for a more reliable and customer-focused alternative to QuickBooks. This experience has been a clear indicator of their lack of professionalism and respect for their customers.

use_case: QuickBooks Online addresses major challenges associated with manual bookkeeping, disorganized financial records, and restricted access to data. By automating processes such as bank reconciliations, invoicing, and expense tracking, it greatly cuts down on the time and effort required for everyday accounting tasks. The platform consolidates all financial information in a single location, which makes it straightforward to produce real-time reports and monitor cash flow. Its cloud-based nature is particularly advantageous, enabling seamless collaboration with team members and accountants from any location, thereby enhancing both efficiency and decision-making. likes: What I like best about QuickBooks Online is its user-friendly interface and the flexibility it offers for managing finances on the go. The cloud-based platform allows me to access my financial data anytime, anywhere, which is crucial for staying on top of my business's cash flow. The automated features, like bank feeds and recurring invoices, save time and reduce the chances of errors, making it easier to maintain accurate records. Additionally, the integration with various apps and services helps streamline operations, making QuickBooks Online a comprehensive tool for managing all aspects of my business's financial health.
dislikes: One thing I dislike about QuickBooks Online is the occasional lag or slow loading times, especially during peak usage hours. While the platform offers a lot of features, the performance can sometimes be a bit sluggish, which can be frustrating when I'm trying to work efficiently. Additionally, the cost can add up if you need access to more advanced features or multiple users, and some of the reporting tools are not as customizable as I'd like. It’s a powerful tool, but there's definitely room for improvement in terms of speed and flexibility.
use_case: QuickBooks Online is solving several key problems for me, particularly in streamlining financial management and improving accuracy. By automating tasks like expense tracking, invoicing, and bank reconciliation, it eliminates much of the manual data entry that used to take up a lot of time and was prone to errors. This automation ensures that my financial records are always up to date, which is crucial for making informed business decisions. Additionally, the ability to generate detailed financial reports quickly helps me monitor my business’s performance and plan for the future. Overall, QuickBooks Online is benefiting me by saving time, reducing errors, and providing clear insights into my financial health, all of which contribute to better business management.

likes: Once you reach the point of needing a bookkeeper or accountant, they will most likely be familiar with QuickBooks as it is the industry standard. This application is ideal for business owners or self-employed individuals who prefer having all their financial data stored in one place. QuickBooks Online has transformed the way I handle my business finances, with its standout feature being its user-friendly interface. This makes it easy to navigate through financial tasks and quickly access and update company data. By integrating with other business tools like banking institutions and payment processors, QuickBooks Online has made my workflow more seamless. Its ability to generate professional invoices, track expenses, and provide detailed reports has been crucial in helping me make informed decisions for growing my business, saving me time and allowing me to focus on business growth

It is vitally important for our oversight, auditing, and management. It has significantly streamlined operations. "QUICKBOOKS IS A SOLID BOOK

likes: QB has basic accounting functionality and is based on a process that starts with creating an invoice. If you are a business which does not send invoices (for every sales), QB is not for you.
dislikes:
Hi,

We are a company delivering physical sport coaching sessions with 800 players coming for sessions every week. We have 4 terms per year so around 800 x 4 = 3200 sales and every sale can be multiple services and products. Customers pay by PayPal, Credit card and PayNow (Singapore) and bank transactions and sometimes combine different products and multiple players’ payments (siblings together for discounts, etc.)

We have 2 major problems with QuickBooks:
1.	No functionality to send sales receipts for bank transactions.
2.	Bad working Match functionality for bank transactions

No functionality to send sales receipts for bank transactions.

QB has a sales receipt functionality which adds one or more transactions to QB. However, when a bank transaction comes in though the bank links, we like to send a sales receipt directly based on the transaction and combine several transactions into one sales receipt. QB lacks this functionality totally and there is a super complicated solution on YouTube which we are not going to use as QB should make our lives easier.

As this is a very simple functionality to build in QB, the question is why it is not available where competitors have this functionality built in. Is QB still upgrading their system or just leaving it as it is with no improvements anymore?


Bad working Match functionality for bank transactions

QB has a sales receipt functionality which adds one or more transactions to QB. However, when a payment comes in the match must be completely and exactly the same as the sales receipt, which is impossible as my customers might pay with different payment methods and multiple transactions will add up to one sales receipt. Also, QB takes the sales receipt date as transaction date which is a big mistake for a system as QB. The sales receipt will mostly be sent after the payment date when we have confirmed the payment has been made. We do not send invoices and we have a website where players and parents register, select, and pay for the services and products. We are sending the sales receipt out of QB after receiving the payments. At this moment we are not able to receive the payments in accounts that are linked to QB as it is impossible to match transactions and sale receipts. 

Another option is to send sale receipt out of other systems and do the bookings in QB based on the transactions, which is double the work for us.

What we need is a simple option to list more transactions in the match which do not have to match the sales receipt but, for example, the name of the customer only, depending on a filter. We honestly do not understand why QB does not have better match functionality as it is now just impossible to match without manipulating the sales receipt to an exact match, which is a ridiculous amount of extra work and goes against all accounting principles.
use_case: QB solves basic accounting requirements, however come swith a load of other issues we were not aware of when we purchased

likes: It has most of the features we need, and doesn't feel like a compromise compared to a desktop app. It's initially easy to set-up and use.
dislikes:
It's extremely buggy, and the support is non-existent. Modules stop working entirely, like the whole payroll system, and it can take weeks or months before the issue is resolved. Think about that for a second.

Their support is horrendous; they do not call back when they say they will, and will never let you speak with a supervisor or anyone who could possibly escalate your ticket. Every time you call back, you're starting from scratch; The bottom-line is, when something goes wrong, no one is ever held accountable, and you are on your own.

Also note that the US and Canadian instances are a completely different product, and so it's difficult to find 3rd party plugins that work with your geographical region.

likes: It's convenient for multiple users to access from different locations.
dislikes:
It's clunky, laggy, and buggy. It's not user friendly or easy to use. Even simple tasks like marking a check as cleared, making edits to a transaction, or saving causes to whole screen to reboot. This results in losing your spot and having to scroll to search for where you left off. In the payroll section, sometimes you cannot submit filings - even though it's just marking it as submitted internally in your account. 

During one of the more recent updates, the option to submit feedback was completely taken away. They continue to make it more and more difficult to connect with them or submit feedback. And, even when you find a way to submit questions and feedback, it is completely ignored. 

Another recent (negative) change, was a complete reworking of the dashboard. Quickbooks Online used to have a flow of operations that made it relatively easy to jump in to any part of the flow you wanted. Now, you have to search for it in the menu and the dashboard contains all sorts of useless information.

There are issues with emailing of invoices, screens locking up frequently, being able to send emails from your own domain (they have to come from Intuit or gmail.com which results in customers thinking it's spam), batch invoicing, etc. Plus, you have to pay for a whole new license pack just for a second company, unlike QuickBooks Desktop. I seriously regret changing over from Desktop to Online.

use_case: To all small businesses do not hire Quickbooks live Bookkeeping. This is how they operate. They will ask you to pay $200 a month for Bookkeeping they will delay your books by 2-3 months so you have to pay in advance 3 + months ahead of time. They will not provide you any financials for your books until 1 year late you will have to get a extention to file taxes. So the first month you sign up. They will delay it out 3 months then turn it into 20 months. Of paid $200 a month services. If you stop your subscription they will refuse to provide you any books for 20 months. This is what happens. I signed up in January 2022. I paid $200 every month the bookkeeper is always 3 months behind. So that's an extra $600 I paid. Now todsy is August 23rd 2023. They are still delaying my 2022 books and still never received my tax package for last year. I canceled my subscription Aug 2023 because they are not sending me any financials for 2022. And now they are stating since I canceled my subscription they cannot work on my books at all or provide me my LAST YEAR financials. I have videos confirming all bank statements were received by QuickBooks and they still refuse to send me my financials until I resubscribe to the service. because I already paid for them. They never provided me any financials for 2022 and 8 months in 2023. Meanwhile the book keeper is in receipt of all my financials. They refuse to send me my books from 20 months ago. They want me to continue to pay $200 in advance to be 20 months behind. This is fraudulent. To intentionally delay the books by 20 months to keep collecting $200 for Bookkeeping service. This is a scam. To make small businesses pay $200 in advance $200 x 20= $4,000 I paid and now QuickBooks is saying because I won't continue to pay 20 months in advance they cannot provide any financials because I need to have a active current subscription in order to receive anything. This is a scam. Why would small business pay $4,000 receive no services then get demanded by quickbooks to continue to pay $200 just so they can delay it 3 more months to continue collecting money for services. They tell you it's $200 a month in Bookkeeping but small business beware. They tell you $200 but they delay your taxes by 1-2 years to keep collecting the $200. And if you cancel after paying in advance for 2 years they will refuse to send you any financials I got scammed. I have paid 20 months of books and quickbooks refuses to send me any documents because I do not have a active subscription. So they want me to pay $500 reactivation fee plus $200 more per month and now we are 2 years behind. Consumer affairs legal needs to be ware of what's happening. Quickbooks is defrauding small business. Small Business Beware Live Bookkeeping

likes: I like the integrations and do find it easy to use.
dislikes: Terrible experience. After over 10 years of using the product I called at end of year to request a discount for the next year as I was being offered far better prices from competitors and considered switching away. Spent about an hour on that call and was promised a 50% discount if I stayed a customer. I agreed but wound up being charged the full amount. When I called back I spent nearly two hours on the phone with them to get that refunded and then they said I needed to pay the full amount again but promised that they'd refund $160 of that per my discount. So I paid a further $320 and then the guy said "oh, I'm sorry, a 50% discount is no longer available, I can only give you a 40% discount". Feels like a complete scam to keep customers by offering discounts then not actually giving them those discounts after they pay in good faith. Be careful.

The only upsides are that it has online tech support, and a lot of accounting firms use it. 

I can't really think of anything positive, so I'll try my best. I suppose it's good for the economy, because rather than being an intuitive, easy to use product, I have to employ tech support and up the amount I pay my CPA in order to deal with this awful piece of junk they call software.

dislikes:
The support is actually unbelievable.
1) Contacted 3 weeks ago about adjusting email addresses for users to migrate to a new domain and it repeatedly gives an error message. Support said they'd escalate and resolve by Monday.

2) started 15 chats about the issue since then, each support rep has no idea that there's a ticket on the account as they apparently can't see past chats so be ready to explain yourself in depth every time and listen to the same basic troubleshooting steps every time. My suggestion is to write out an essay once then copy and paste into a new chat to save you time.

3) one support rep sent over a screenshare software Glance that wouldn't open after downloading, instead of providing troubleshooting steps to find out why it wouldn't open or sending over an alternative the rep said "what do you want me to do?" And then ended the session wasting an hour plus. 

4) support reps are also incredibly quick to close chats, even if you are waiting on them to look into an issue on their side they'll randomly send over a "are you still with me?" Message and 60 seconds later end the chat forcing you to connect to someone else and wasting another hour. 


TLDR; it has been an absolute nightmare trying to do something as simple as changing the email address in manage users. Support unable to assist for weeks, quick to provide you the same basic troubleshoot steps and then close their chats.

bonus_data.title: Easy to use, easy to set up

likes:
- Easy to set up: QuickBooks is easy to set up and start with. You can create an account in minutes and start tracking your finances right away.

- User-friendly interface: QuickBooks has a clean and intuitive interface that makes navigating and finding the features you need easy. Even without accounting experience, you can quickly learn how to use QuickBooks.

- Variety of pricing plans: QuickBooks offers a variety of pricing plans to fit your budget. The basic plan is affordable and includes all the essential features you need to manage your finances. If you need more parts, you can upgrade to a more expensive plan.

- Great integration with other software: QuickBooks integrates with popular software solutions, such as Salesforce and Google Drive. This makes it easy to keep your accounting data up-to-date and accessible from anywhere.

QuickBooks is an excellent option if you are looking for an easy-to-use and affordable accounting software solution.

likes: QuickBooks Online's best feature is undoubtedly its easy-to-use platform. The software is easy to navigate and the interface features a streamlined dashboard with intuitive menu controls, so you can quickly generate the reports you need to make better decisions without wasting time searching through content in multiple tabs or windows. It also handles tax compliance, such as VAT filing, very effectively by providing automated systems so SMEs don't have to worry about managing their taxes properly each quarter or year.
dislikes: I do not like that QuickBooks has long waiting times for customer support. Although the compilation of data is extremely beneficial, waiting on hold can be tedious and inconvenient at times. The monthly fee associated with this add-on can be disproportionately high for customers who do not accept credit cards regularly, especially if you only use the credit card processing feature occasionally.

use_case: Managing invoices and expenses

likes: I was trying to keep track of my income and expenses to stay organized. However, since going online with QuickBooks, things have become easier. The simple to use interface and the simple to mobilize platform make managing funds very streamlined. I find the invoicing feature very important, which quickly provides and sends professional looking documents to my clients. The ability to keep track of payments and debits on a real-time basis also changed the rules of the game for me. I no longer need to worry about entering written information or trying to keep track of collections. QuickBooks Online has made my business efforts easier and allowed me to focus more on growing my business.
dislikes: The user interface can be quite confusing and complicated, especially for those who are just starting to use accounting software for the first time. I consistently take a long time to search for the procedure or document I require.
use_case: Generally, the experience I have had with QuickBooks Online has been very positive. It is a powerful tool that has accompanied me in managing my finances and tracking my company's costs. The user interface is simple and easy to navigate, which helps me get started and use the program correctly. I also like the ability to customize the documents and tables to suit my particular requirements. Also, the customer service has been first class, with welcoming and helpful representatives who are always free to guide my assistance with any issues or complications I have recognized. I strongly recommend QuickBooks Online to anyone who wants a workable and effective accounting solution for their company.

likes:
Accessibility: QuickBooks Online is a cloud-based software that can be accessed from anywhere, as long as you have an internet connection. This makes it easy for business owners and their accountants to collaborate and work on the same financial data, even if they are in different locations.

User-friendly interface: QuickBooks Online has a simple and intuitive interface that makes it easy for users to navigate and manage their finances, even if they don't have a background in accounting.

Automatic data backups: QuickBooks Online automatically backs up all your financial data on a regular basis, which helps to protect your data in case of a system failure or other unforeseen event.

Customizable reports: QuickBooks Online provides a wide range of customizable reports that can help business owners and accountants analyze their financial data and make informed decisions.

Integration with other applications: QuickBooks Online can be integrated with a variety of other applications, including payment processors, e-commerce platforms, and CRM software, which makes it more versatile and useful for different types of businesses.

Scalability: QuickBooks Online offers different pricing plans and features that can be customized based on the needs of your business, which makes it suitable for small businesses as well as larger enterprises.

Time-saving features: QuickBooks Online includes features such as automatic bank feeds and transaction categorization that can save business owners and accountants a lot of time on manual data entry and reconciliation.
dislikes: Limited customization: While QuickBooks Online offers a variety of features and functions, some users have noted that there are limited options for customization. This can be frustrating if your business has unique needs or requires specific features that are not included in the software.
use_case:
Manual accounting: QuickBooks Online eliminates the need for manual accounting tasks, such as data entry and reconciling bank statements. This saves time and reduces the likelihood of errors.

Financial tracking: QuickBooks Online provides an easy way to track financial data, such as expenses, income, and invoices. This helps business owners to stay on top of their finances and make informed decisions.

Invoicing and payment processing: QuickBooks Online allows businesses to create and send invoices, as well as process payments online. This makes it easier for customers to pay invoices and helps businesses to receive payments faster.

Financial reporting: QuickBooks Online provides standard financial reports, such as profit and loss statements and balance sheets. This helps business owners to get a clear picture of their financial performance and make informed decisions. QuickBooks

likes:
Quickbooks online has an easy to understand interface that makes regular bookkeeping tasks easy to pick up and accomplish, even for new users. It also has great reporting and advance features for accountants and others so business owners, admins and regular users can work together to see the big picture and keep an eye on 
a business's cash flow and finances.
dislikes: There are some items you cannot do as a "batch" (must do one at a time) and as an accountant user, that can be a bit tiring. Especially when there are other things you CAN do in a batch. I believe QBO is trying to protect it's users from inadvertently making big changes quickly, but as an accountant user, even though they already give more leeway I think a bit more is necessary is some places.
use_case: QuickBooks Online is a great asset to all our clients, no matter what industry their business is in. Sometimes they just need to track transactions from month to month, others need payroll, billing and the whole accounting gamut - and it is able to do all of these things and make it easy for them, or they are able to have us do it and provide them with clear and concise information about how their business is doing or what changes could benefit them each month. It's nice that it's in the cloud so we can both access it and help each other when necessary.

bonus_data.title: QuickBooks is great, whether you're just starting out or your business is booming

likes: I have used QB for years in different businesses. They have been nice because they integrate with my bank accounts and I can easily reconcile my bank accounts. However, I WILL NEVER USE THEM AGAIN.
dislikes: Quickbooks makes a hard push to use their "payments" option with your customers and with invoices. Then, when your customers use them as the option, they put a "hold" on your account. FOR WEEKS. We have provided documentation repeatedly for two weeks. Then they send out an email demanding documentation. This is for a four year-customer who pays us weekly for some services and on an invoice that was created in November, and has made several payments since then. On April 4, our client paid the remainder of his invoice, $214,617. In spite of multiple hours on the phone, multiple uploads of the same documents, and multiple "a supervisor will call you back," quickbooks still has our money. We don't have it. Our customer doesn't have it. Quickbooks stole it. And we cannot make our payroll. DO NOT TRUST THESE CROOKS WITH YOUR MONEY.
use_case: They are not solving problems for me, they are creating huge problems for me. If it was possible to do negative reviews for support, I would. T heir support is pathetic, and useless. Quickbooks

I appreciate that Quickbooks is a simple-to-use tool for recording the financial records of a small business. The ability to add documentation to almost all transactions also helps immensely with keeping a paper trail of the decisions behind certain actions.

dislikes:
Quickbooks sorely lacks consistency in training its support personnel. This is problematic when issues in how the software is designed interferes with one's work process and the source of assistance cannot provide the required help. At times you will get a GREAT knowledgeable clerk; at other times you will get more help from a Google search. 
Also, Quickbooks help forums sometimes contain conflicting solutions or partial assistance. 
The inconsistent support is one of QBO's biggest flaws; I have heard complaints from experience bookkeepers to CPA firms. Some of whom have taken their business elsewhere, especially for Payroll.

Once a task is complete that requires you to drill down several clicks they seem to think that what you'll want to do is jump completely out of everything and back to the main menu. likes: The biggest benefit of using Quickbooks Online is its reliability and convenience. By moving to a cloud-based system, clients can easily access their data whether they are onsite or offsite and collaborate more efficiently with accountants, suppliers, employees, and more. Updates come automatically, so users always have the latest version installed without having to worry about manual upgrades or downloading outdated software. Its intuitive design makes it easy for even novice users with little accounting knowledge to perform basic functions such as sending invoices and tracking expenses.
dislikes: I find QuickBooks' current client and invoice/estimate selection process cumbersome; when selected, a particular client's phone number is not displayed without hovering over a miniature phone icon. It would be beneficial if there was an option to receive email notifications when paid invoices are received. Also, I feel that the date-based sorting on the home page should be extended further - each invoice or contact listed should correspond to its specified date.
use_case: QuickBooks helps me save time and be more efficient with my financial records. Generating statements at the end of each month or accounting cycle used to take a lot of effort, but QuickBooks has streamlined this process so that I can allocate my resources to other tasks.

bonus_data.title: Great tool for online book keeping

likes: What users like most about QuickBooks Online is the ability to quickly and securely manage a business's accounting needs from any web-enabled computer or device. This versatility allows for maximum convenience, as tasks can be completed at home, on vacation, or while traveling with only an Internet connection. In addition, its intuitive layout makes it easy to engage in financial housekeeping without prior experience with this type of software. Despite its many advantages over desktop versions, what really sets QuickBooks Online apart is how easy it has made tax filing by connecting directly to third-party tax services such as TaxAct, which streamlines year-end filing immeasurably compared to other traditional methods of bookkeeping.
dislikes: I wish QuickBooks would hide the phone number when selecting a customer or invoice/estimate as I find it distracting. Instead, focus on the small phone icon to make calls when needed. I would also like to receive an email notification when invoices are paid, but this feature does not appear to be available at this time. Furthermore, all invoices and customer interactions should be arranged chronologically on the home page itself rather than having to perform separate searches each time.
use_case: My business finances are easily tracked with QuickBooks, allowing me to keep track of where I stand financially. It saves me time from having to manually compile reports each month or accounting cycle, allowing me to track data more accurately with less effort. This helps improve the accuracy of forecasting future financials by providing a simple system that stores past performance.

likes: QBO makes invoice management easy with an easy-to-navigate UI and analytics dashboard. Without even needing to click any buttons, I know exactly how much is overdue and can send reminders with the click of a button.
dislikes: The payroll wizard was sufficient for our startup needs, but as we neared 50 employees and consistently onboarded 5 new hires per week, we needed to transition to a more comprehensive and accessible provider for our employees. More often than not, the invitation link sent to new hires would expire or QBO would not let them create an account. As a business, we had representatives that we could contact for help, but our end-users are not paying clients and therefore had difficulty finding adequate customer service help. Most of the issues were related to the backend of QBO and were out of my control as the administrator, so we also needed to move to a payroll provider that gave administrators more troubleshooting control.
use_case: We use QBO for all of our accounts receivable and expense management. We reconcile the activity in QBO with spreadsheets of income/expense records managed by cost centers across the organization. The spreadsheets have varying degrees of accessibility by people in the organization, but in QBO, we control access so that only the administrators have the ability to view and manage income/expenses. QBO gives administrators the ability to reconcile bank activity without giving them access to the bank accounts, which has the most strict access policy.

likes: I use Quickbooks for its supposed transportability of business income and expense information that ideally speeds up tax prep and lets me store, review, and sort related data. I think the app looks nice in general, and I like its styling.
dislikes:
I dislike the inability to transport data into Turbotax - This function routinely fails, and the resolution is to enter the info by hand, negating the app's main functionality. Furthermore, the app is difficult to use in the following areas:
- Sending tax info to TurboTax -- The main reason I subscribe to Quickbooks is that it makes it keeps track of tax information that may be imported into TurboTax. This saves time because I don't have to input tax information by hand; however, the import functionality has not worked for at least two years in a row. The first year I was told it was because I tried to file too early after receiving an email that I was eligible to file. This year the issue is unknown.
- Invoicing -- It's difficult or impossible to delete an invoice, change a sender's info, or otherwise update the invoice. It's also not possible to see what the invoice you're sending looks like before you send it, and the invoices seem to get stuck in spam filters. I'm not sure what email they are sent from. Also, I often need to send invoicing info to multiple people and the invoicing system only provides a BCC, not a CC. 
- Mileage -- The mileage tracking system is cumbersome, and it does not save info or make entering round trips easy. The auto-tracking feature assumes all mileage is for business but only a fraction of my mileage is.
- Performance -- Reviewing transactions is sluggish and often lags and registers screen presses out of sync with screen updates, leading to incorrect selections.
- Support -- Help isn't there when you need it. I used the help feature from TurboTax to receive a call right away when the import failed; however, they do not support QuickBooks and sent me to QuickBooks support, which was not open or accepting calls. I only found out they were unavailable after navigating the automated support menu that attempted to walk me through the steps leading to the failed import.
- Exporting to TurboTax -- At the risk of belaboring the point, exporting does not work. Not only does it fail, but you are only allowed one attempt at exporting. The solution given in the error notice and by the TurboTax staff is to enter the information by hand, which is unacceptable.
use_case: QuickBooks Online supposedly makes importing tax-related income and spending data to TurboTax faster. It also stores that information in one place, making tracking business goals and metrics easier throughout the year.

likes: I use Quickbooks for tracking job cost expenses via the check register.
dislikes:
Not sure where to start right now. 
1. Support has been outsourced and they do not understand the product. My payroll was not working correctly. The support person told me I would have to delete ALL of my payroll and rebuild it. That might have been an option in January but this was September. ARE YOU KIDDING ME?
2. The cost has gone up significantly (probably to pay for the outsourcing)
3. If you need to move to a different company, or close your business, UNLESS you pay $554 per year, you will lose all of your data. I don't understand as the LAW says you have to keep it for 7 years and if you have used Quickbooks for any length of time, you will need to print boxes and boxes to comply with the LAW. 
4. I have paid $325 - $355 per year for many years to use Quickbooks. This year, after lockdowns due to COVID, and with so many businesses trying to stay in business, Quickbooks raised its price to $554 per year. ARE YOU KIDDING ME.
5. When you attempt to call the only phone number listed as a US Office: 650-944-6649 the recording says they are not accepting calls. 
6. If you are lucky enough to get transferred to a supervisor when you call sales or support, they say they will listen but will not share your concerns as that is not their job.
7. They do not care about their customers in any way. It appears they are possibly catering to the larger customer.
