likes: I liked that I could send invoices to customers abroud, until this happened ...
dislikes:
My customer paid the invoice I sent him, he received a receipt, money was transferred to my Quickbooks account, and after a day or two of processing, they were deposited to my bank account. The customer received services he paid for, and a week late I see that the whole transfer amount was deducted from my bank account. I didn`t receive any explanation at all, I didn’t get any email from QuickBooks whatsoever.

I had to call them and spend 2 hours on the phone to hear that cause of the currency difference they had to refund the amount to a customer. And they just suggested that I contact the customer and ask him to pay again. 

I thought that if you pay monthly fees for a payment system, then once this system accepts, processes and deposits the payment it means that it accepted, processed, and deposited the payment. How come they can come a week later and say that there was an error and now you on your own have to get your money back? They definitely do not protect either the seller or the customer. They protect just their business and all “your bank account is safe with us" is such a joke. 

How do I know the same situation won`t happen to me again? If the system processes the payment and deposits it to my bank account, how can they just take my money back and say that there was some system error? Sounds like a robbery and a reason to call a lawyer to me

bonus_data.title: Quickbooks took money from my bank account without any consent

likes: The awful experience I have had over the past three months with Quickbooks Customer Support has wholly negated any potential upsides for working with Quickbooks.
dislikes:
I have owned a small contracting business using Quickbooks for several years. I recently decided to set up Quickbooks Payments to allow my customers to pay their invoices via credit card. There was a processing fee that came with each transaction, but I decided that the convenience of quick payment was worth the cost. I was utterly mistaken. 

The first credit card payment made to my business was processed in a reasonable timeline. The second payment, made a few days later, was held up by Quickbooks for review. They requested additional information be sent to them (bank statements) to verify my account. This request was completed within a day. Since that point nearly three months ago, I have called Quickbooks on four separate occasions to get some explanation as to why my payment was being withheld. Every conversation I had with their service team took over an hour. No one could give me an actual explanation, but they always guaranteed me that the payment would be released within two business days. It has been almost three months, and I still have not received payment or an explanation. 

The lack of communication, direction, and, most importantly, the payment has caused me to lose all faith in Quickbooks. These actions are causing my company to be on the brink of dissolving. 

Please, take my advice and avoid Quickbooks at all costs. 

Regretfully, 

A disappointed business owner.

bonus_data.title: Good potential but you have to become an expert

likes: On the accounting side, QBO for years has been the standard for ease of accounting. Our accounant with 30+ years XP swears by it's ease. The UI is actually decent and for the most part the software works like it should. There are some nice features that work together although limited (like customer historical data working with Projects, PO's etc).
dislikes:
Need to be able to enter more products about data (BOM, Weight, Dims, Alternative vendors and P#'s, etc). If you estiamte/invoice is too large then the system runs very slow (10 - 15 seconds per click before that action you click works). 

Quickbooks and Intuit support on a whole has declined over the years as the companies focus has been less on the customer than thier profits, superbowl stadiums and ads. We've ran into a few issues over the years with little to no resolution including a failed implementation becaue of a non deletable line item that should be easy to bypass. Also the template forms are limited in functionality (can't edit a packing slip, no sales orders), and they make it hard to implement an outside payment processing system and constantly push ads to use thier services (ads on a Paid service). This system is not good for tracking inventory as well.

If you plan to stay small in size then QBO may just fit you, or if you ONLY plan to use accounting that may be fine. Our proces relies a lot on working within QBO and i've been trying to find alterantives, but unfortunely QBO is built to "lock you into thier ecosystem" so that you lack the functionality to, say, export your data....that is yours...for a serice you pay for......and you can't export it after all that. These are just a few of the complaints we have with Quickbooks / Intuit.

Explore other options like Wave, Odoo (open core/source erp software), Xero, and others if you can.

likes: Where shall I begin, ok when I began using the online version of Quickbooks in 2013 I was so lost. The desktop was all I had used and many different desktop versions. I had issues with the transactions and reconciling and it seems to me that now in 2022 those 9 years were a huge deal when it came to listening to the people that use it every day. No more transactions coming over in triplets, you are able to unreconcile a bank or credit card account now thank goodness, there are a lot of features that weren't always there before and I have to say I am very pleased with the flow it has to it now and as a bookkeeper that helps to manage time better, complete projects sooner and CPA's meet tax deadlines without the need to file an extension on their clients behalf.
dislikes: The main thing I have an issue with while using the online version of Quickbooks is when you are trying to multi-task it is a bit time consuming. The reason for this is because you have to close things out that you are working on to get to another section of Quickbooks for instance, if I am looking at a balance sheet and I need to look at the profit and loss as well, I have to go back to the reports to open the profit and loss and doing that closes the balance sheet. Then I have to go back to the balance sheet by hitting the back arrow. But once there I always have to re-enter the dates. That has always been an issue that I thought they would fix by now and they haven't. Other than that everything else is great to work with during your busy day!!
use_case: Having the online version just makes sense. There are apps now that integrate with it, you can log in on your phone, the data is easily accessible to both the client and the accounting team eliminating either one of them to have to leave their office. It's just all around a great business tool QuickBooks

likes: That they calculate tax rate quickly. Coming from Zoho Books that is literally it.
dislikes:
Their customer service stinks. Their systems and processes need serious improvement. I had a client pay me 2 weeks ago and I am still waiting on these funds. I have had to pay out of my pocket to cover the inventory. Let me just walk you through this nightmare:

1. Client makes payment 11/23
2. Funds held for review 12/1 saying they don't have all my information. Requested EIN, phone bill, utility bill etc.. I had just provided this information to them a week prior. Within 5 minutes sent the requested information back. Spent over an hour on the phone with them trying to figure out what is going on. This information has already been provided to them. One person doesn't know, another person says that it is because my passport was not scanned correctly? Why didn't they ask for my passport again in this request then? They backtrack on that and say nevermind that isn't the issue. Just be patient. 
3. Funds should have been released on the morning of 12/5. Chatted with someone who said that I didn't submit my documents right away and that was the delay. Not true. They confirm they currently have everything they need and to wait a few more days. INSANE! I have been waiting almost 2 weeks at this point. 30 minutes later I get an email saying they don't have any of the information that I uploaded and they are requesting it again. They updated the requested document date, which on the system shows the review day being pushed out another 2 days.
3. Chatted with THREE different agents. Each said they would transfer me to the correct department. Each time the chat ended and nothing happened. 

This is my first few weeks experience with QB ^ Coming from Zoho Books I have had literally 0 issues. We are a corprorate gifting platform and in order to purchase inventory for clients I need a fast deposit. Luckily we were able to front the inventory for this order but I no longer have trust in QB. We will likely be closing our account. I cannot imagine this happening to a client where I couldn't afford to purchase their inventory upfront. It would absolutely ruin our reputation and be beyond embrassing. I cannot trust them to be our merchant servicer. I literally have no idea when our funds will be released. They don't care about small businesses putting them in difficult situations like this. A quick Google review will yield hundreds of stories just like this. Some of them wait months upon months. Very disappointing.
use_case: They are currently causing more problems than issues they are solving. They don't care about small businesses. Funds held.

dislikes:
It is exceptionally complicated to make simple fixes to my accounts in Quickbooks Online. Intuit also puts advertisements for more Quickbooks services into my online Quickbooks dashboard. Advertisements that appear in front of and above my accounting reports.

I regret having switched from Freshbooks to QBO. Freshbooks provided all the functions I needed and introduced none of the miscategorization problems that I often encounter in Quickbooks.

The biggest two problems QBO has introduced into my business are 1) lots of time is spent fixing accounting errors 2) my late payments rate has increased on recurring invoices.

1) I don't know why I have so many duplicate entries, so many expenses and receipts in the wrong accounts, nor why it is so time consuming and difficult to fix these problems. I often find myself reading 2-3 QBO help articles, just so that I can address one simple problem and fix it.

2) Clients must make an Intuit account (or login to an existing Intuit account, which introduces confusion) to setup recurring payments. Clients should be allowed to simply enter a credit card. Stripe can do this easily. QBO should do this too. There should be no need to make an Intuit account to pay my invoices.

I am disappointed with QBO. It's a constant source of frustration and it wastes so much of my time. I wish I had not switched to Quickbooks.

None of the upgraded features that I have tried (for example, using the Pro plan, employee time tracking) have worked for my business because these tools are so difficult to use and because they introduce so many errors into my accounts. For example, duplicate entries, auto-categorization rules that miscategorize expenses, auto-categorization rules that turn on without my having approved or created them. Quickbooks Online is Disappointing and Creates

dislikes:
They do not stand behind their mistakes. A software company is only as good as its ability to take responsibility for its actions. When Quickbooks was first started out I bet the owners didn't want to own a company that is irresponsible to its clients. I have been using this Quickbooks before it was called Quickbooks. 
I had a problem where Quickbooks failed to send 941 payments but said it had in the software. Now they want to use every single excuse in the book to defer and said its not their fault when it clearly is. They fail to see that I have paid them for multiple companies for over 10 years and they want to lose a customer for $100 worth of fees.

likes: Relatively straight forward in terms of implementation for a new or pre-existing business.
dislikes:
Quickbooks has created a closed ecosystem that all but forces you to use their payment merchant services (no native Stripe or other 3rd party processor integrations). I recently did a QBO deployment for one of our businesses and enabled online payments for a new invoice that we generated. QBO had no problem adding the online payment link to the invoice, which the customer subsequently used to make payment. This is where the fun started.

Only once the client payment had been successfully received, did I get a notice from QBO that the deposit to my account had been put on hold until they could "verify" my account / business information. Fair enough, I understand the requirement and had gone through similar processes with several providers before. However, after submitting our (very straightforward) documentation requirements, I was informed that Intuit had denied our merchant application. No reason given. No opportunity to rectify. Just denied. In addition, the customer payment that triggered the verification would be held for 270 DAYS! as a "security deposit". Here's the response from their 'support':

"Alright, A bit of bad news. Intuit closed your account as a business decision. These decisions are final unfortunately. So with the payment you have processed. You would have to have the customer dispute the payment with their bank or card company. Then take the funds another way or it will sit on the account as a security deposit for 270 days. Then you would have to reach out 271st day to receive the funds. I am sorry for the inconvenience and I do apologize. Unfortunately, Where Intuit is considered a low risk processor, and sole proprietary company. They don't have to provide notifications for closures or give the reason for closing the account as a business decision. I am sorry."

So I'm left with 1 of 2 choices: Ask the customer to try reverse the payment from their end (not a great look) and try collect payment online through the clunky, non integrated payment processor options in QBO. Or go cap in hand to Intuit in 271 days time to ask if they wouldn't be so kind as to release the (significantly sized) customer deposit that decided to unilaterally hold onto with no information, clarity or recourse.

In summary, be careful of deploying QBO if you want to collect payments online. I can't recommend this solution for anyone beyond a 'mom & pop' style business.

likes: I can access QBO from anywhere as long as I have an internet connection. I travel for business, but am never out of touch with my accounting system. This feature makes QBO much better than the standard desktop/server option. My CPA also has direct access to my QBO account whenever he needs it. The process is much more seamless than the desktop/server option.
dislikes:
QBO lacks many features that are available in the enterprise version I ran several years ago. I miss being able to completely customize purchase orders and invoices. We now have to settle for the forms provided by QBO with minimal ability to modify. Frankly, the POs and Invoices do not look good. Also, the available reports and ability to manipulate the reports was much more comprehensive in the desktop version. I used reports on open and all purchase orders on desktop. The reports in QBO don't meet the other standard.

I also hate paying a monthly fee for QBO. I'm paying $80 per month. That adds up quickly. Previously we purchased a new copy of QB enterprise every few years and were done with the expense.
use_case: We handle all our accounting functions on QBO. The ability to work remotely means that project managers can input costs in real time instead of waiting weeks to return to the office. This gives us a much clearer picture of our financial status.

You cannot add or edit your company name on an invoice. The invoice literally says "No company name". I can't send it to clients like that. I tried to add a company name through entering it in 'first name' and 'last name' but that didn't work. Then I tried following the QuickBooks online forum but "Edit work info" button wasn't an option. Then I reached out to customer service but he took 10 minutes to pull up my account then just kept telling me to "hang tight while he pulled up my account". Umm, this isn't a complex issue. I just want to access the BASIC feature of putting my company name on an invoice. 

I have never had such hassle with a basic feature. I signed up for Freshbooks and it was SO EASY to create an invoice. I asked QuickBooks for a refund but they responded that the fact you can't actually use the software isn't grounds for a refund, haha. That just proves my point about customer service (and I asked for a refund the same day I bought the service, and they can clearly see I didn't use it since no invoices were sent). Horrible product and horrible service.

AVOID Intuit QuickBooks unless you want to lose hours and hours trying to get your money!!!

My client paid $3,000 in January and QuickBooks refuses to release my funds. Instead they inexplicably canceled my business account (“we don’t have to tell you why according to the agreement you signed”) and refused to relinquish my funds!

Today I wasted ANOTHER hour and a half fighting with a chat agent who merely said, “we will review your case within 270 days, and either re-review or release your funds to the state.”

EXCUSE ME???

I am BEYOND FRUSTRATED with them. THREE TIMES I’ve spent hours on phone or chat and they rattle off some policy that says, “We can screw you any way we want.”
 
I rarely post negative reviews but I’m actively campaigning this time so others don’t get suckered out of their hard earned funds. 

I feel violated. 

PLEASE do yourselves a solid and avoid these crooks!

#thieves
#crooks

I've emailed the gentleman who initially sold us the product, and he has rarely returned my emails and never returned my phone call. We also purchased the payroll support and were charged twice for the initial $500.00. Despite reaching out to our salesman, we have not heard back or received any acknowledgment that the problem will be resolved. I understand things happen, but everyone has email on their phones these days, and can easily see when someone has emailed. To not respond is rude and unprofessional. 
We were told we would be given a virtual assistant to help us with clean up, audit and general set-up of our online version since we are transitioning from the desktop version. I'm hoping and praying this is true and once we can finally complete the online setup.

use_case: For affordable, simple and secure online accounting purposes, QuickBooks Online is the real deal.

likes: It gets the job done. It's nice that you can have customers pay you directly through QBO. I like that it can work alongside other apps to push and pull content.
dislikes:
My most significant pain point with QBO is the permissions. We create 'projects,' and the Project Managers cannot see the expenses on the projects they manage. A setting turns off their ability to see the companies check registers/accounting (which makes sense) but that doesn't necessarily mean they can't see expenses for managing jobs. It's a huge flaw, in my opinion. Only Admins can see the expenses and run reports to see if the job was profitable. I (and others) have reported this issue for years, and they still haven't fix this.

The bugs. The custom templates have a bug that hasn't been fixed in years. They randomly have updates, but there are usually new bugs that arise that you don't know about until you find them. The IT support is hit or miss.
use_case: Just the general accounting problems and reporting. I like the custom and automated report options. Also, the ability to push and pull content to and from other apps (Quickbooks Time) is nice.

bonus_data.title: It's good but it has bugs and those can be frusterating

likes: On the most basic level possible, QuickBooks does have a good interface. It's relatively clean looking and they also have decent promotions.
dislikes: Once you start getting QuickBooks extensions, a lot of them simply do not work. What makes this so difficult is that they have arguably the worst customer service. Their customer service will simply transfer you between departments, over and over and over again, and none of those departments will be correct. I signed up for QuickBooks Payroll Premium to simplify my accounting processes. This ended up being one of the worst mistakes I have ever made because it now takes up hours out of each day just constantly battling the software and their "support" teams to try and get things to function the way they are supposed to, and 9/10 times they can't figure it out. So what has ultimately happened is I get charged about $100/month to pay my contractors and QuickBooks has made it impossible to actually get them paid through the system. Instead, they just charge my card every month and then tell me someone will be reaching out to me from the correct department to resolve the issue. They never actually reach out... It is the most frustrating service on the planet and I would stay away from it at all costs unless you are sticking to the basic level subscription only. If you are considering using QuickBooks for payroll, direct deposits, or anything else, just don't do it. Save yourself the headache.
use_case: QuickBooks Online is currently not solving any problems that benefit me. In fact, they introduce NEW problems to me on almost a daily-basis. I signed up to help make my accounting and financial reporting processes easier, but it would be easier to use cash and keep my books using a pen and pencil. My Advice: Avoid

likes: Being online, it allows you to connect to your bank account so that you can perform reconciliations with just a click. It also allows you to upload documents so that you can keep a separate copy online for easy retrieval. The UI is very user-friendly, that allows even a person without much knowledge on the system to be able to use it without issues.
dislikes: The only thing that I do not like about Quickbooks Online would be the inflexibility to amend the currency for a vendor that had been created, for example, to change the payment to the vendor from USD to SGD, which requires us to create a separate vendor account. Other than this, it seems that the classifications under e.g. "current liabilities" and "current assets" are different throughout different geographical areas e.g. in Singapore, in Indonesia and in the USA, perhaps it's to cater to the local GAAPs, but it presents a small bump for the importing of the chart of accounts.
use_case:
I've solved the requirement to set aside computer data space to maintain our own offline software and also the time needed to do reconciliations as well as time on the retrieval of documents and reports. Time is of utmost importance and QBO saved us a number.

Having multiple users and also having the ability to include users for "reports only" greatly reduces administrative hassle. Key personnel can access and retrieve reports as and when needed with the relevant filters to that they can obtain the information that they need, when they need it without needing to go through a 3rd party.

dislikes: The customer service is very frustrating. The chat function is a bot that attempts to solve customer questions, but it isn't as helpful as a human who can listen and diagnose human questions. The chatbot also doesn't function properly 100% of the time which adds to the frustration. When calling the hotline, there is an automated system similar to the chatbot. It takes a long time, and when a human finally answers, they don't have the decision-making autonomy to help. The reporting function for payroll could use some improvements. When navigating through employee information, you are often prompted to return to the main employee page after leaving a screen so you can't quickly bounce from one employee information page to another.

likes: The hold time for a customer service agent.
dislikes: I didn't like how my company's were treated. We opened 6 quickbooks online and payroll accounts, I was sent fraud emails immediately after running my first payroll. We sent over all documents requested and accounts. After 48 hours a email was sent for each business stating our businesses are high risk for fraud and they'll be closing our accounts and won't be reopening them EVER, after speaking with several reps, no one knew anything and couldn't explain to me what was happening. I couldn't pay my employees for 2 weeks and had to meet everyone and apologize for the inconvenience with paper checks. All the reps kept saying is after they verify the payroll account everything would be fine. After a couple more days the entire Quickbooks online account was deactivated an error messaged popped up saying my login is invalid and no account was found. Rep stated send in the list of documents again to verify my account. Did that and it took another 2 days to verify. Finally the account got unlocked. 2 weeks of unexplained delays, no apology, no payroll explanation, just mistreated for being a serial entrepreneur and black owned business.
recommendations: I didn't solve anything I'm looking for another software to accommodate my businesses.
use_case: Not made for people of color

likes: I like how multiple users can be in the system and updates are saved right away. It's easy to manage multiple clients and vendors through the system too for a small-medium size company.
dislikes: The basic needs of this stream is great, for the most part. Some pieces you'd think the system would be capable of - it's not. We send out 80-100 invoices every two weeks, and there is supposed to be a link attached for easy direct payment option, however, when we send invoices in bulk, that link doesn't send. This has been a known issues we've tried to resolved numerous times but the solution is to send those invoices individually that need the link. We also are unable to send remittances via email, but instead print and send manually which again, seems like we should be so far past that point but still the way it has to be done. If there is not very high volume, it's manageable still though.
use_case: We are able to manage multiple customer accounts along with clients so the two sides of our business. The reporting functionality is also pretty great to get breakdowns in different areas of the business. QuickBooks Online for

dislikes: Not reliable so far. I cannot access my payroll. The first seven days was fine. All of the sudden, I cannot access any portion of the payroll. I spent two hours chatting with the agent. They could not resolve the issue. I am contemplating going back to QB desktop. Support needs to be improved!
recommendations: The biggest benefit is that the program is online. I can give my grant writer report access without compromising confidential data that she does not need to be privy to. The program saves time by linking your bank account and very quickly issuing payments to vendors. It enables you to give your accountant access so that you do not have to send your data backup via email. This keeps data more secure than through email. Not very impressed.
use_case: Quickbooks online is not the same program s the desktop version. There are similarites but it is not as user friendly of a program.

reviewer_name: Verified User in Hospital & Health Care

bonus_data.title: QBOE has no meaningful support anymore

likes: It integrates with our bank. Downloading and matching is now about 90% accurate, but bank-QBO outages still happen maybe once a year, usually after the bank makes changes -- and this is a tremendous improvement. The user interface continues to improve. We were very early adopters of QBO, moving over from Quickbooks Desktop, which was anything but user friendly, and at the beginning, QBO wasn't nearly as good but they have steadily improved it. You can usually get helpful reports out of it. The reconciliation feature works well. It has proven to be a stable platform. My daughter has a Master of Accounting and her observation is that for the non-accountant, QBO is a good tool but accountants don't like it because it is so different in design from the desktop. For our staff, it is much or accessible than the account-friendly desktop version and being able to log in remotely and easily makes collaboration much easier.
dislikes: We have had some really bad experiences with tech support over the years. I am the oldest digital native you can imagine. I am 59 and had a computer when I was in high school in 1980. I have spent hundreds of hours on customer and tech support over the years. I was certified in Apple Server management. I am fluent in Adobe, MS Office, HTML. I programmed DBase 4 and Filemaker. I was an early adopter of CheckFree before Intuit bought them out. I have run Quicken in several iterations, Quickbooks desktop and Quickbooks Online. My daughter is a forensic accountant in law school. So I speak with authority when I say that Intuits customer is about the worst, on average. With QBO you pay for tech support as part of your expensive subscription. What this means is that you can talk to an offshore person working from their home, which was true even before the pandemic. If you need a call back -- which I have on several occasions -- there is a minimal chance you'll get one. Their VOIP is unreliable. It couldn't call my phone (believe me everyone else can). They don't call back when they say they are going to. You can never rely on the front-line folks. ALWAYS escalate to a supervisor to reduce wasted time. They won't use email to contact you if they can't get VOIP to go through. They have a proprietary interface (in the same vein as zoom screen share) which is a tech support nightmare in and of itself. Thankfully, the software is not usually buggy, but if you need help, it won't be there for you, and accounting software is complicated enough that an online forum is not the way to solve the bugs that do exist. And did I mention YOU'RE PAYING FOR THIS SERVICE? I would NOT go back to the desktop version under any circumstances. However, if you can find a different package, I strongly urge you to consider it first, second and third, and if all else false you may be, like so much of the world, stuck with this horrible monopoly.
use_case: When it works, it works quite well. And that is 95% of the time. Four percent of the time you can fix the problems it has yourself. The last percent is hopeless because you have to contact customer support.
reviewer_name: Verified User in Mental Health Care

I am constantly amazed by companies who choose to denigrate their product and service by using poorly trained call centers. They should be training them to solve/transfer/escalate the issue within 3 minutes without hold times.

likes: I love that QuickBooks Online is easily accessible from any device, anywhere in the world.
dislikes:
Over the past five years being a QuickBooks Online customer and user for multiple businesses, there are four major things I dislike:
1. The monthly price has randomly increased multiple times without providing additional major features.
2. The quality of the support has decreased, which is understandable as the number of users has greatly increased, but the monthly price continues to increase.
3. There are constant popup windows trying to sell me on new services, or funding opportunities, or their latest product. 
4. The amount of downtime is significant and has been very inconvenient.

use_case: I have recommended Quickbooks Online to others working in the nonprofit space, and I will continue to recommend it. This software has been instrumental in keeping our organization financially intelligent and healthy. Once an organization grows to have multiple revenue streams, including earned income, Quickbooks Online, without question, should be part of your technology/software portfolio. Quickbooks has made our audit process seamless and painless as well. Quickbooks Online has been exactly what our organization needed as we have grown exponentially over the past three years.

likes: I do enjoy the way it can be set up to automatically track your spending and have the possibility of automating the system towards your needs. However, you still have to work on QBO and pay $25+ a month to do so.
dislikes: I wouldn't say I like that it's costing the user $25+ a month for simple accounting purposes. The other 'added' features like accepting payments via CC and ACH are all free on other gateways, such as Melio Payments and Square.
recommendations: Bookkeeping is the main reason I use QBO. Capturing purchases from respective bank/CC accounts takes one task away from others. When I need to accept payments, I only use it as a secondary option, as I usually use Melio Payments and Square. At this price point $25+/month: You work Quickbooks
use_case: I would advise you to open yourself to using multiple payment gateways such as: Melio Payments and Square, just in case QBO doesn't meet your actual needs of accepting payments. Melio Payments offers both no-fees for accepting ACH payments and taking CC payments with you getting the finance charges or relaying the said charges to your customer (giving you both the option). On the other hand, Quickbooks Online does have a pretty straightforward payment system where it allows you to send payments directly to your customer via email or create a customized link for your to share in-directly with your customer. It also allows you to turn on/off toggles for accepting credit card and ACH payments, which is another nice feature. All in all, Quickbooks online is a good for all types of purposes, but for account purposes by itself, it is overly priced.

likes:
As I mentioned in my title, Quickbooks customer service is awesome. I struggled at first to get the hang of QBO, but whenever I was in doubt, I would simple call QBO, and with basically no wait, a CS agent is always available to help. I sometimes do screen shares so they can walk me through certain scenarios. They will stay on the phone as long as it takes until a solution is found.

From a product standpoint, it can be quirky on occasion, but with some patience/practice/use, you realize how powerful it is. Quickbooks also offers events that help brush up on how to use their software, new tax changes, etc. They offer credits for those too, which is a nice bonus.
dislikes: The initial learning curve was steep, but that said, I am not an accountant. After a few months, I really got the swing of things (thanks in part to their great customer support).
recommendations: We are creating beautiful reports with QBO, which our investors appreciate. Everything is so customizable. Quickbooks just makes day-to-day bookkeeping easy. While I don't use it, they will even auto-learn ("rules") how you like to categorize transactions.
use_case: The developers of Quickbooks really stands by their product. They offer elite support, which made a steep learning curve not so steep. They also have a great online community to help answer complex questions.

I upgraded my software from Quickbooks Simple Start to Quickbooks Essentials. They gave it back in the form of credits for my next purchase. I cancelled my other QB software subscriptions and trying somewhere else!
use_case: Why does it matter? I quit this secret subscription charging service

likes: Since Intuit has brought Quickbooks to the online cloud world, there are many things that online fixes from what Quickbooks desktop offered. The first is stability; the online environment is much more stable. The second is visibility, in that transactions, ledgers, billing, payroll, debits, and credits are very easy to identify. The ability to perform tasks in the online version once you get used to the interface is effortless to work within. The best part of the online is sharing and providing specific access that allows for both accountants and clients to interface on the same materials at the same time. You can also run as many clients or engagements as needed with various subscription options. Their online chat help has improved a lot.
dislikes: What Quickbooks Online is missing are some of the reporting that Intuit used to provide as part of the original Quickbooks Desktop, such as in-depth Acrural and depreciation. They also need to find a better way to extract specific asset classifications into a report for uses of tax filings. For example, if you specifically have your assets in a classification, you can run a report to get the specific assets, but it doesn't allow for multiples at a time, and is a bit heavy handed. Also conversions back and forth between Online to Desktop or vice versa are an all or nothing type of process. You cannot select specific years or date ranges to import or export. A roll back feature would also be useful for errors made.
recommendations: The biggest problem has always been that constant annual work in a Quickbooks Desktop file makes it work slowly over time. There is more data, and the files grow too large and require rebuilding or repair. By converting to Online, it does away with this. The benefits are also enormous by not having to renew constantly. We've realized we're more secure because we know who is in control of the materials and who has access.
use_case: If you have been struggling with Quickbooks Desktop in your environment, this is a more cost-effective and easy-to-use solution for you and your clients or accountants. Consider if you have been working with usage and upgrades to Quickbooks Desktop endlessly. The best part of Quickbooks Online is that it allows you to see what needs to be fixed in your accounting more clearly, and the integration of other online systems like Bill.com or Expensify is effortless to do. I recommend Quickbooks Online if you are looking for a refresh to what you are doing for your accounting.

likes: The best part about QBO is the ease of use. The software is clutter-free and straightforward to navigate. Unlike many software, where there are options under the options creating a much complex environment, QBO is very simple. It has pretty much everything a company would in its accounting software.
dislikes: Its simplicity is also a disadvantage here. QBO is very basic and does not have in-depth functionality if that is what is needed. Like inventory management function is there, but it does not have any process to assign a location to the inventory. For in-depth functions, one has to go and use QB Enterprise, which is a desktop version. However, they have the option to host and make it available on the costly cloud. I also found that QB Online is much easier to access remotely as the situation demands these days- work from home.
recommendations: During a pandemic when everyone was working from home, we were impacted because we used a desktop version. We explored other software and finalized on QBO only because it provides a Payroll function. Handling payroll is one of the most challenging situations in an organization, and QBO makes it a breeze for employees to log in and download their paystubs and forms. It helps our accountant to manage taxes to be paid to CRA and at the same time give errorfree -accessible paystubs to our employees.
use_case: If you do not want to use multiple software to manage your business, then QBO is an excellent option. It is essential software with the ability to do everything but in a limited capacity. However, the best part is its pricing. It is one of the most economical options available today to own accounting software.

switched_from.reason: Our version of Sage is a desktop version of the software. It is good but very traditional. They failed to evolve with the market where the needs are everchanging. The software is also complicated as the user interface is not very good.

likes: QuickBooks is one of the most popular accounting software around, and for good reason. There are tons of resources available to help you get started or troubleshoot issues that may arise in your business. Some great features include inventory management, accounts payable/receivable, billing/invoicing options etc., which can be customized depending on what type of company you're running.
dislikes: The initial interface for accounting can be difficult and daunting without a background in the field. I am not excited about being locked into monthly fees, having to pay transaction costs every time you send or receive money, and needing an instant payout which comes at even more of a cost. likes: The features of the platform are great! They truly make it easy to keep your books in order. Their app used to be good as well, not sure why that started getting less and less featureful and stopped working as well.
dislikes:
I have used Quickbooks products for 15 years now and have been happy with everything they've put out up to a couple of years ago. As they started coming out with new products like QB Cash I was quick to sign up and start using them. They promised great features that I was excited about; however, that's when everything became a dumpster fire for me. They started getting rid of features on their mobile app, one of my other businesses used QB Self Employed, and they started getting rid of features there as well. QB Online began to have a lot of glitches, wouldn't load at all, would freeze, would quit randomly as I was building an invoice and had to restart, etc. And I tried multiple devices, browsers, etc. The app also barely worked anymore to the point I stopped using it all together. I tried to sign in the other day, and it literally took 10 minutes just to try and sign in to my account from my phone because of loading times, pages crashing, etc. 

Now for QB Cash, I actually lost money through that. I tried to make a transfer, and the system glitched, and it didn't go through, but then they charged me a fee because of it. When I tried to get help, no one could help me. I got transferred to 3 different departments, and none of them handled QB Cash. One person FINALLY was able to tell me it's not even handled by QB, it's by a third-party bank which is fine, but even the customer service and online portal was supposedly handled by them. So I called them, and they didn't know what I was talking about; they didn't even know what QB Cash was. So I kept calling people and FINALLY got to someone who said they handled QB Cash, and I shared my issue, and surprise surprise, she said she couldn't help either. But at least she was able to submit a ticket to her support team and said she would get back to me - that was more than six months ago. 

After that, I was charged TWO MORE FEES all for something that wasn't even my fault. So I tried to get all of my money out of QB Cash and close the account, but for two weeks, it was glitching every time and wouldn't let me. So I called in multiple times, and still, no one could help. I finally figured out a work-around with PayPal and got my money out. Then I had to pay back all the fees because they were about to put me in collections, and I didn't have time to spend hours fighting with people who just kept telling me they couldn't help. 

Then when I tried to cancel, two accounts went just fine, but my third has been a mess. I canceled it on my own through the settings like my other two accounts, but that didn't work. So I spoke with someone to have them cancel it for me, and to my surprise, they not only canceled it but refunded my last payment! That was two months ago. Then this week, I got a notification that QB tried to charge my card. Thankfully I froze that card so that they couldn't charge me. So I called in and was assured the account was canceled, and I wouldn't be charged, but of course, today, I was charged. And had to spend another 30 minutes getting that fixed. 

I could go on and on, but bottom line, I've spent hours and hours on chats and on the phone with QB support because their product has become so difficult to use with all the technical issues, and their customer serivce has been terrible except for a few very good agents. On top of that, I signed up at $35/month, and over a few years, it was raised to $53/month, AND their payment processing fees have gone up considerably as well.

So I switched to WaveApps that is FREE and has more reasonable payment processing fees and does everything QB does - without the glitches.
use_case: Accounting for my business, and they did a great job of reporting and helping me see exactly how my businesses were doing. Used

likes: QuickBooks Online has been a long-time leader in the accounting field. Well established, easy to use, and understand. The program integrates with many other applications very well, making your transactions easier to complete.
dislikes: Personally speaking, one of the most significant drawbacks that I have found with QuickBooks Online is the limited number of accounts users that you can establish on the program. Compared to other online accounting programs, for some people, the price will be an issue, but with the features offered in this program, this can easily justified the cost of the program.

likes: I liked having access to our records from anywhere.
dislikes:
We migrated from the desktop/on premise version of QuickBooks to the QuickBooks Advanced online version several years ago. While it has been nice to login from anywhere, the functionality has been far more limited with the online version. Simple things like creating great estimates or tracking our customers by state and rep have been painful or impossible to get right. 

The estimates are extremely limited in their functionality. We wrote a special connector to bring the customer contact data from our CRM to QuickBooks, and special templates to try to make QB estimates work, but we could never get them formatted in a way that was sufficient. There were common fields like line-item discounts that it simply did not support. Worse, there was also no adequate helpdesk assistance to be found. 

The system also requires the use of special fields for some simple things that were standard in the desktop version. Segmenting customers by state or sales rep were some of those items. And, now that I want to downgrade to a less expensive version with more limited licenses, from my current $1,920 per year QB fees, I’m being told that the lower cost version doesn’t support these special fields, so I’ll lose all that data. These fields should be part of any accounting software at any price! 

Lastly, the support is just plain painful. The people are always nice, but they rarely have more than the most basic answers. That’s mostly because of the limitations of the online version of QuickBooks. And, forget about ever talking to development. They are located in a blackhole somewhere. You can email them, but you will almost certainly never hear back. 

Overall, I’d call Intuit QuickBooks a--formerly wonderful--failed product. I’m actively looking for ways to abandon it all together. I do not recommend it. The value proposition has soured.
use_case: It does do accounting.

likes: The software, generally, is well-designed and works pretty well most of the time. They do, occasionally, have issues which require contact with them to resolve, however...
dislikes: Several times in the last weeks and months (Summer 2020) I've tried to get ANY sort of direct support from QBO. I've spent literally HOURS waiting **unsuccessfully** for an online chat where the heading says, "We'll be with you in 5 minutes." This is mid-week, during business hours! They have no phone support, not an option. If you already have a QBO expert available to you, you are golden, but for anyone who may occasionally require support, you are out of luck!

likes: Without any doubt, QuickBooks online is a great software that was launched with an aim to help small and medium-sized businesses to maintain their accounts. It has a very user-friendly interface, especially the dashboard is quite awesome. When it comes to the reporting section, Quickbooks online comes with the ability to generate instant important management reports. For instance, whether you need a report of trial balance, receivables, or payables, you can easily make it in Quickbooks online. Besides, you can automate the sales invoice generation process which will save you a good amount of time. Furthermore, as it is a cloud-based app, you can access it instantly from anywhere in the world if you have an internet connection.
dislikes: In my opinion, Quickbooks online is not very effective for larger organizations. Besides, it is a little bit slow compared to SAP or Tally.
recommendations: I have been using Quickbooks Online in our organization for a good amount of time. It helps us with daily, weekly, or monthly reporting. It also helps us in calculating VAT and preparing VAT returns. Besides, we use this software for sales invoicing to our customers. On top of that, it is cloud-based software, so my team members can easily access it remotely in certain situations. Great Accounting Software for
use_case: I would happily recommend this product to you if your business falls into the category of small and medium-sized enterprises. In fact, it will be a great accounting tool for your organization for up-to-date reporting.

switched_from.reason: Quickbooks online has more user-friendly features and relevant reporting options compared to Freshbooks.

likes: For those companies and organizations looking to improve their accounting software Quickbooks is a great solution. While not as intuitive and easy to use as systems designed for non-accounting professionals, Quickbooks offers a myriad of features and functionality that is an accounting professional's dream.
dislikes: For the most part, I assume that the one using it is an accounting professional, capable of understanding the inner workings of AP, AR, etc. For this reason, I would not recommend the system to those who are not familiar with accounting procedures and systems.
use_case: A major problem with some accounting software is that the reports required for analysis, verification, etc. Are not avaliables. QuickBooks' extensive report customization is a great solution for this that has resulted in an overall positive experience with the software.

likes: We use QBO for multiple businesses and one specifically where we fully take advantage of the Estimate, Invoice, and PO functions. This all works seamlessly and helps us to stay organized. Best that all those features are in one single service allowing us to work quickly. Being able to quickly view the status of the business from a web browser or phone app, makes things a lot less stressful. Having an accountant keep all the information can sometimes be tough depending on how much you need to interact with your data (like categorizing expenses, or managing estimates, invoices, and PO's).
dislikes: I dislike the cost and the fact that you must pay a subscription plan for each individual business. This can be tough for smaller businesses just starting out. When compared to the desktop version, it is a one-time fee and the desktop version can host multiple businesses. To try to manage these costs, we have moved some of our QBO instances onto the desktop version run by our accountant. QBO cloud features are not all necessary for standard accounting. So if you need simple accounting, I would stick to the desktop version, or better yet, try to use the desktop version and let your accountant manage it.
recommendations: Outside of standard accounting, we are able to buy and sell products using the Estimates, Invoices, and PO functions of the Plus plan in QBO. To have all of these functions in other accounting services is not competitive. These tools help us track costs across the business. Being able to convert estimates to invoices, and then in invoice settings be able to charge a percentage of the total or a percentage of individual line items offers a lot of control and flexibility. This helps when billing customers/clients for either products (buying wholesale and selling retail) or for services (allowing to charge for a deposit up front and then charging for the remainder when the job is complete). Really
use_case: Make sure you really need an "in the cloud" solution. If you're simply trying to categorize expenses, give the desktop version serious consideration, much cheaper.

likes: The business snapshot and the instant reports that can be generated are probably the best feature. You're able to tell at a glance how your business is doing. Sort of like taking its pulse. The reminders are helpful too, especially with payroll, and monthly and quarterly tax reporting. It also syncs with your online state and federal tax payment portal and you can pay your quarterly and monthly taxes with a click. You can run any number of a plethora of reports available ranging from P&Ls, Balance sheets, payroll costs, accounts payable/receivable, etc.
dislikes: Not a lot of things can be edited. Specific line items in invoices (eg. SKU can not be edited to UPC, etc). Not very beginner friendly - took me over a year to truly master it. The chat feature for support doesn't work half the time, and it takes up a LOT of ram - so much so, that you have to close down the window about 20 min into your session just to clear the cashe or it will freeze up. If you want to add payroll, automated payroll, live bookkeeping, etc - That's also extra. Generally it could be slightly more user friendly.
recommendations: I've gained the ability to have an instant pulse on my company and know who owes me at a glance, and can customize the home screen to show my revenue per month or quarter and where I stand currently with A/R, A/P, and payroll etc.
use_case: If you're not familiar with GAAP or Accounting in general, I'd highly suggest brushing up on. them or taking an accounting course before jumping into Quickbooks Online.

likes: Quickbooks Online provides a simple, easy to use interface with powerful enterprise-class features. It helps us keep our expenditures organized, preventing us from allowing money to slip through the cracks. Running a small business requires a ton of work, but making our customers dreams come true makes it all worth it. Building and maintaining customer relationships is time consuming. It leaves very little time for administrative tasks like accounting. Quickbooks allows me to maximize my time spent with customers building brand and business, and minimize time spent on important but mundane accounting tasks.
dislikes: While I find incredible value in Quickbooks Online, I wish the performance were faster. I often find myself click and waiting, clicking and waiting, etc. When I get my brain in an administrative mindset I can move very quickly, and I wish the software would keep up. Opening an expense to edit a field and save takes longer than it should. I have even tried using the Desktop app version of Quickbooks Online, which is supposed to increase speed, but I still feel like it performs slowly.
recommendations: Before we switched to quickbooks, we had a manual process for tracking business spending. While cost effective, this method was tedious and we would often find we had missed expenses because they were charged to odd accounts or we misplaced receipts, or forgot properly reimburse expenses purchased with personal money. Keeping all our data in Quickbooks has completely changed the game. The subscription costs are easily paid for with the tax savings we enjoy by not missing expenses.
use_case: Take full advantage of the receipt snap feature which allows you to quickly enter expense receipts on the go from your phone and helps automatically match those receipt pictures to the correct expenses!

likes: <v ->My name is Hope Sinclair.</v> I'm the executive director of a nonprofit organization. And I definitely rate QuickBooks Online a 10. With QuickBooks Online we're pretty much solving all of our financial problems that we're having. We're able to keep things organized inside of QuickBooks Online. It prints out great reports for our board meetings. And what's really good is it aligns with ADP TotalSource, so I don't have to do too much maneuvering or adding numbers because ADP TotalSource generates a report directly into our QuickBooks Online and everything's in one setting. So one of the things that I like best about QuickBooks Online is the reports that print out for our board meetings, prevent me from having to do things that I normally would have to do manually. They also help with our treasurer reports that our treasurer has to give to our board. And then at the end of the year, when we're doing our audit or being audited and turning in our 990, all of our reports are right there in QuickBooks Online so it's not like I had to do too much finding the papers and the receipts and things because you can actually scan the receipts right in QuickBooks Online and everything is right there. So one of the things that I dislike about QuickBooks Online is the fact that it doesn't make money for me, (laughs) which isn't really a dislike because no program would technically make money for you. But the fact that QuickBooks Online pretty much is that one-stop shop where everything is right there, I really don't have any dislikes. So one of the recommendations that I have for individuals who are considering QuickBooks Online is to definitely make sure that you try the desktop version first because you'll never go back. So QuickBooks Online is the, I can pick it up anywhere. So I don't have to be in front of my computer. I can literally be home, log in, and it's right there. So the recommendation I have is, if you try the desktop version, you won't try it again. You'll definitely go to
It is very easy to use and integrates well with several other products, such as MineralTree and SalesForce.

likes: What I liked best about the software is that it's easy to use. You don't need to be an accountant in order to know how to use QBO. I was able to record journal entries and find financial statements fairly quickly. I'm able to work fast because if I needed to replicate a journal entry for a monthly use, I am able copy the journal entry from a prior period, all I need to do is to date the dates. I also like how you can double click an account and there is a breakdown of all the numbers you need to know. Reconciling the bank has never been easier, you just record your journals and check off the boxes and as soon as the difference becomes zero you are good to rec the bank or account.
dislikes: What I dislike most about QBO is when I was trying to look for a certain journal entry/transaction from the general ledger. It was very hard because when it's a big file and you're going line by line sometimes you lose your spot. QBO does not highlight any of its transactions and sometimes, I would have to start over from the top which was a little annoying. Also, I noticed when I'm at a certain spot on the general ledger and I click on a transaction and want to go back to the previous page, it jumps back up all the way to the top and I lose my spot again. Another problem with QBO is you cannot open two things at the same time. I have tried and I always lose one of them off the screen. I have tried to login into QBO, one on Google Chrome and another on Internet Explorer in order to compare two financial statements side by side, but it would log me out. It would be great if that could be fixed.
recommendations: The problems I'm solving with QBO is reconciling the bank monthly for our clients. We also do bookkeeping and record all of their transactions for a certain entity, in order to get their financials ready for year end for when we need them to file their tax returns. We also do quarterly bonus calculations, where we need to use the financials for our compensation model. The benefits that I've realized is, QBs automatically calculates all the numbers for you, you just need to record them correctly. It's less work for you were originally working off an excel. Also, when i need to pull financials I can just export to Excel or PDF, which is very convenient. It usually takes seconds, but with bigger files, obviously it's going to be a little longer.
use_case: QBO is one of the cheapest accounting software out there. If you are down between Sage and QBs, I'd choose QBs or QBO. It's almost the same, but there's price difference.

likes: My favorite aspect of Quick Books online is that it is flexible and portable, and travels easily with our business. Previously using QuickBooks on a desktop I struggled with having to be in office to update records such as receipts. The Receipt scanning feature is indispensable. The mobile app by far is our favorite feature. while we have an office, we are a mobile focused business and being able to do our accounting work on the go is very valuable.
dislikes: As with most accounting software's there are always challenges. While I actually believe Quick Books software is the best out there. I think it lacks the ability to customize views and templates. Also as opposed to the desktop version you are subject to a good internet connection and the software working online. Occasionally we are unable to load or save a new invoice on the go. Pricing is also a little steep . $70 a mo for the plan we are using, is significantly higher than other options. But I still believe it is the best on the market.
recommendations: Qick Books online is solving all of our bookkeeping needs in a portable and powerful platform.
use_case: make the plunge, at least the trial. You will be impressed!

likes: When we first started using Quickbooks 10+ years ago it was simplistic and you really had to compromise when switching over from the desktop version. But that is no longer the case. Over the years they've made it a complete and competitive accounting software with all the bells and whistles. In fact, there some of the features are better on the online version, like recurring invoicing for services. The add-on credit card service is integrated and trivial to use, as is the add-on payroll. On a week with no modifications/expense reimbursements we can do payroll in about 15 minutes. The best feature, though, is the syncing to our banks and credit cards. Because it's constantly updating we always know the status of our bank accounts. It remembers categories for vendors/expenses we've previously taken the time to customize so that keeping up is very easy. Monthly reconciliations are easy because it's all already done. You basically just double check that both sides balance and you're done. They have something like an app store to extend the functionality of it and some of the additions are extremely useful. We bought one that allows us to import bulk invoices from a CSV so we can generate hundreds of invoices with a few clicks, based on an export from our ticketing system.
dislikes: Support can have a long wait time. It really depends on the day/season but on a bad day you could be on hold for an hour. Thankfully their help knowledge-base is very useful. We often find the answer to what we needed while on hold. The credit card processing add-on is a little on the expensive side if you use it very little. It has a monthly fee whether you use it or not, so it's not cost effective if you sell through credit cards seldom. On the other hand, if you're constantly charging customers with a credit card it is more competitive than our previous 3 credit card processing companies. We actually stopped using QBO for card processing because of the monthly fees multiple times and every single time we ended up coming back to them because the ease of use justifies the expense to us. We rarely use it, though, so it's really not geared for us or companies that, like us, mostly get paid through checks. Lastly, the number of allowed users is a little limited. They just expanded on this but even now it's not enough for what we wish. Ideally we'd like to give limited access to technicians and sales reps so they can generate quotes. The quote to email system is fantastic, but it requires a person with accounting access to generate them. We've been asking for this for years and we've been told is under development still.
recommendations: We do quotes, invoices, payroll, bank reconciliation, check writing/payments and to a minor degree inventory tracking. The switch from desktop to online increased the speed with which we do payroll, reconcile and invoice. Everything is organized in an easy to understand layout. A business owner without a background in accounting can very quickly navigate through to get things done. The biggest benefit has been being able to see a real time snapshot of the company's finances. In the desktop version we were always behind, waiting for information to be entered. We had to look at last month's financial data to predict this month's. But with QBO you see live data and can make better decisions for your business.
use_case: Don't wait! Try it. You'll never go back to desktop accounting.

switched_from.reason: We originally just wanted to do payroll while on the road. That was the selling point. Originally it was really limited (12 years ago) but it's grown significantly. It is not better than anything else we've tried.

likes: I love being able to easily track my business income and expenses, see my profits, and calculate taxes.
dislikes:
Getting everything set up was pretty hard as I wanted to include my income and expenses going back to the beginning of the year and had to import transactions from some bank accounts and credit cards I was no longer using. I also wasn't quite sure how to categorize everything at first, and made some mistakes that affected the overall totals. 

I also accidentally duplicated some records while setting it up and ended up having to delete all the data and start over in order to get it to calculate correctly (fortunately I had previously been tracking income and expenses in a spreadsheet so I had something to compare to for accuracy). I finally ended up paying my CPA for an hour long consultation to log in to my account with me on a Skype call and answer all my questions, help me set things up, and confirm that I was categorizing everything properly.

Since getting it up and running, using it has been a breeze and I'm very thankful for it in my business.
recommendations: My business income is sporadic and comes from a variety of sources, and QuickBooks makes it easy to keep track of all of it in one place. A huge timesaver after
use_case: It's a lot easier to start using QuickBooks at the beginning of a fiscal year and only track future income and expenses rather than trying to backlog income and expenses from before using QuickBooks. Also, it's worth hiring a CPA to help implement complex use cases, but once it's set up, it's very easy to self-manage.

bonus_data.title: Great way to track finances for our startup

likes: The accessibility of a cloud based software and convenience of software pairing to help with efficiencies. It also has the benefit of being saved on a cloud where we do not have to worry about losing data saved on a server or hard drive. There is also the ability to track our program expenses and grant expenses using the profit and loss function for customers. There is a lot of customized reporting which helps with end of month reconciliations and our monthly Finance committee meetings.
dislikes: Not as easy to navigate compared to QB Desktop, sometimes runs a little slow, and sometimes have issues with the linked bank accounts syncing. I also feel as if it doesn't have quite the capabilities of desktop online and does not look as clean. I do feel like there is one more level of tracking that cannot be done with QBO. We have to track grants, programs, events, and general admin expenses. I wish there was an easier way to do this all at one time. For example, we may need to track information that was specifically for an event, but that was funded by a grant, but also need to see how much of the funding was restricted vs how much is unrestricted and it's not easy to pull that all encompassing report. Financial statements are also not easily pulled based on the new accounting requirements for non-profits. These statements have to be prepared with two columns (unrestricted and restricted). There is no capability to do this within Quickbooks--it has to be transferred to an excel spreadsheet.
recommendations: Tracking expenditures for our programs and grants simultaneously. Realized that there is a simpler way to do this without having to pull it all into excel.Reconciliations for bank statements, credit cards, and pre-paid debit cards have proven to be faster with the online banking & credit card syncing tool. This tool even memorizes certain categories based on prior transaction entries from prior months making it even quicker to reconcile a bank statement.
use_case: Make sure to take accounting courses before navigating Quick Books. It is easy to learn, but also easy to mess up financial statements if you don't understand what you are doing within the software. It is good for those who have to work remotely frequently and maybe do not have an endless budget for backing up data on a server. I would also advise that if you are not an accountant by trade to have a CPA or tax accountant as a resource to ensure everything is recording correctly within Quick Books as it will allow you to enter accounts and make changes that aren't necessarily accurate or in your company's best interest. This is a really affordable software for the average small business or organization.

Not ideal for a growing company that has more complex or demanding reporting needs, or for accountants who are looking for more options within a system

likes: <v ->Hi, my name is Deborah.</v> I'm a director and a partner in a company in the retail industry. I would give Quickbooks Online a nine out of 10. We use Quickbooks Online for all of our bookkeeping and accounting needs. It's great because our accountant can log into it whenever he or she needs to, get the information they need to prepare reports, to prepare taxes, to keep track of the business. All of my daily bank transactions and credit card transactions are downloaded into the registers in Quickbooks, which makes it simple, at the touch of a button, to reconcile your bank accounts and credit card accounts. You can invoice, you can receive payments. It's just a really fantastic accounting and bookkeeping tool. It's also got, which is my personal favorite, a sales tax module. For those of us who pay sales tax on a monthly or quarterly basis, it's an invaluable tool. What I like best about Quickbooks Online is the fact that it's web-based, I can pack and take it with me. I get the most up to date versions, so I'm never lagging behind in anything. It's very user friendly. And I don't come from an accounting or bookkeeping background, so that's very important to me. The sales tax module, in particular, makes it simple to calculate and track sales tax payments, and it also makes it simple to record them and keep them in a place where it's easily found. There was just so much good about Quickbooks Online from the automatic downloads to the check registers to the reconciliation, to the sales tax module, to the invoicing, to the purchase orders. It's really just the best little tool for small and medium sized businesses, and probably even large corporations. Although there isn't much to dislike about Quickbooks Online, there are a few things that I think could stand improvement. It's a little awkward, sometimes, between moving between different functions, such as receiving payments and then depositing the checks. I mean, it's silly that you have to go from one place to another. It's not that big a deal, but it's a little annoying and I often wonder why they don't tweak that a bit. There are also some things that occur when updates happen. You'll suddenly log in one day and where your received payments or another function might be, you see something totally alien to you. Their support used to be a lot better. That's also gone downhill, but the good thing about it is they will stay on the line with you until your problem's solved. So there's a few tweaks, but I think they could do a little bit better with a few things. But all in all, I'm very satisfied. If you're considering Quickbooks Online, my advice would be to work with your bookkeeping and/ or accounting professional closely. It's good to know that you'll have their support, that you will be on the same page with regard to how it's set up, how to set it up properly, how to make sure your chart of accounts is on the same page. You may wanna use the presets, you may not want to. We didn't. We created our own custom. It worked fine. You will also wanna compare it with other softwares for your own sanity, although I think you'll find that, for the money, Quickbooks Online is tremendous. They also have plans where you can get help from a professional setting up the Quickbook account online. You can also get extended and in-depth support from Quickbooks. They also have many different plans, depending on what your needs are, so it's really beneficial to work with both Quickbooks and your accounting and bookkeeping professional before you buy this product. When you do, I'm sure you'll be very pleased.

likes: QBO is a straightforward, easy to use accounting system that allows complete tracking and reporting of essential items, storage of payment backups, and multiple simultaneous users. While it is not the most robust system available, it certainly does the job when basic accurate accounting is needed, a short learning curve is crucial, and reports of various kinds can be intuitively created. QuickBooks works the way most people think. It has very little jargon (there is some, for example a "purchase" implies an item was bought, a "payment" is more general) which makes it easy for the less trained user. And if you do get lost, there is a very convenient help screen. Video tutorials are available through the help screen.
dislikes: QBO is robust enough to run almost any business, even one that has multiple locations or profit centers. It lacks the ability to track the items in your inventory if you have a business that sells product, but it will track the value of inventory. If you create a separate account for each item you sell, and if the value is constant, then I suppose it can track the number of items, if somewhat indirectly.
recommendations: The need to quickly and easily produce reports of different kinds based on the receipts and expenses to many different operating units of our business was simplified through QBO. As a nonprofit, we received many grants for particular operations. And each donation and expense needed to be tracked and reported frequently to keep all the volunteers informed. A previous online software we had used was cumbersome and not at all transparent or intuitive in helping to create the needed reports and track the individual units. Short Learning Curve the Result of an Easy to Use,
use_case: This is a great piece of software. It is very versatile and easy to use. It will not do everything. But any small business will find it a great help to understanding and tracking their business work.

likes: QuickBooks easily saves me over 40 hours a year when it comes to keeping my finances organised. My bank accounts and credit cards are synced to the platform, so transactions are synced automatically and take me a maximum of ten minutes a week to look through and expense. Quickbooks's transaction categories are the same as the ones HMRC use, so when it is time to file my tax return I simply export my yearle income statement and copy paste the numbers into the HMRC website. I used to dread anything to do with bookkeeping before this, but as a sole trader this is easily one of the top three tools that keeps my business running smoothly. The mobile app is well laid out and the monthly fee is worth it in gold.
dislikes: I dislike that the mobile app does not have a search function to look through transactions.

The company's customer service and support is lack luster @ most. There is no clear number to call and they always push you back to online questions even when it is evident that you want to speak to someone due to dissatisfaction. The ONLY time I got a number was when I entered the process to cancel. Here is the transaction about my cancelation process:
Here is where we are at..
1.	On the 7th I had a conversation with a person named Janice or Janine via CHAT and we went through the process and she was supposed to cancel the account. This WAS NOT COMPLETED as I was still seeing attempts for you all to charge my card. which lead me to..
2.	On the 16th I had a conversation with Aldrin Dave C via chat whom confirmed for me that the account was NEVER closed by Jannine. He offered to close it as well and process a refund. Then was abruptly disconnected from the chat.
3.	I then called support and spoke to Kristen who then told me that I had to pay first and then a refund will be processed. I then asked to speak to a supervisor who then told me the same and I went and updated my billing information SO THAT I can then be refunded. Upon being transferred back to Kristen she told me it would take 1 day and that she has “set a reminder for her to cancel the account the following day and process the refund”.
4.	The 17th came I waited until after noon and nothing. No email telling me it was canceled and the account showed as active in QBO. I then got ON THE PHONE AGAIN with Carol. Carol told me it shows as Pending Cancelation and she does not know why. After further dialog with her and on hold multilple times she tells me it will be done midnight.
5.	Today the 18th @ 9:00am I checked and STILL SHOWS CANCEL PENDING..
I want this account canceled IMMEADIATELY and refunded the FULL $460.10 I was charged! I am not going to use it and have not used it from even PRIOR to the renewal date. On the 7th I got a backup prior to speaking to Janine.
If you would like a copy of the audio files of the calls and chat transcripts please let me know I will be happy to oblige. If this is beyond your scope please forward to someone who is at the level to make this happen. Otherwise I will begin the campaign to notify everyone via my media channels as well as personal channels of this experience with you company.
