Their response even to this:
Thanks for getting back, Marc.

My deepest apologies for the unwanted experience you have went through. No worries, I'll look into this and will inform all those representatives involved and send feedback to them.
Regarding to your concern I will be investigating as to what have happened. Also, regarding to cancelling the account immediately, to set your expectation, all accounts in Quickbooks Online that are subscribed at some point - when canceled it will not automatically take affect but will be on the next billing date but I'll try to check with our Senior product specialist if they can do something about this. However please don't keep your hopes up as I might not do justice to it.

For the mean time, please take care.
recommendations: My business' need for a accounting software that included invoicing on a regular basis but the amount would vary. Horrible Support and they Trick you into
use_case:
BE VERY CAREFUL when you are ready to cancel. 
Here is my experience:
1.	On the 7th I had a conversation with a person named Janice or Janine via CHAT and we went through the process and she was supposed to cancel the account. This WAS NOT COMPLETED as I was still seeing attempts for you all to charge my card. which lead me to..
2.	On the 16th I had a conversation with Aldrin Dave C via chat whom confirmed for me that the account was NEVER closed by Jannine. He offered to close it as well and process a refund. Then was abruptly disconnected from the chat.
3.	I then called support and spoke to Kristen who then told me that I had to pay first and then a refund will be processed. I then asked to speak to a supervisor who then told me the same and I went and updated my billing information SO THAT I can then be refunded. Upon being transferred back to Kristen she told me it would take 1 day and that she has “set a reminder for her to cancel the account the following day and process the refund”.
4.	The 17th came I waited until after noon and nothing. No email telling me it was canceled and the account showed as active in QBO. I then got ON THE PHONE AGAIN with Carol. Carol told me it shows as Pending Cancelation and she does not know why. After further dialog with her and on hold multilple times she tells me it will be done midnight.
5.	Today the 18th @ 9:00am I checked and STILL SHOWS CANCEL PENDING..

likes: QuickBooks Online is a very robust and comprehensive accounting software package. One of the great features of the program is the scalability depending on the size and needs of the small business and the price point changes with each upgrade. There is the ability to add Payroll as well as the Intuit App store to adapt the program to your specific businesses needs. A key feature of QuickBooks online is that it is developed to streamline & automate the small business accounting process as it connects to your bank & credit card information thereby reducing a lot of manual entry. It also allows connectivity online with your CPA/Accountant to help facilitate the processes of accurately maintaining records & preparing for annual tax returns.
dislikes: QuickBooks Online is a much different program and QuickBooks Desktop editions. It is difficult to make the switch from a mostly manual process in Desktop to more automation & connectivity through the Online program. There is some look and feel similarities, but the 2 programs are more like cousins than siblings. It is important that a thorough review of your business accounting needs and plans are reviewed before purchasing either Online or Desktop so that you don't need to make a switch mid-stream which is complicated and costly in time.
use_case: QuickBooks Online is helping my clients get control of the bookkeeping. When my clients are utilizing an easy to use system like QuickBooks Online, their records are in better order for reporting and tax processing saving me time and them money in fees.

The best part of QB is that it does not allow others to play when it comes to managing the returns of their efforts.
You do not need to be an accounting professional to work with QB, but a little tinkering with the software gives you an idea of ​​the operation and will guide you gradually to become an expert in managing your financial affairs or even supervising a basic experience. Staff members will quickly learn how to handle financial matters if it is a small to medium-scale business.
Password systems at several levels guarantee security and prevent data manipulation by other users. With long years of improvements and with feedback from multi-level users and a variety of exchanges, QB has become the leader in this category.

use_case:
Whoever is paying attention, my simple advice is this: DO NOT USE QUICKBOOKS. They are absolutely, positively, the WORST BUSINESS I have ever dealt with in my life, in any domain. My troubles started a few months ago when, after having spent countless hours entering all my company’s transactions in its lifetime, I was not able to login. I called in about this and they told me a number of things to do, but it wasn’t solved, clearly, the first time, nor the second, nor the third, nor the fourth. They made me try everything and jump through every hoop. Every time they assured me that the problem would be fixed. To no avail. They made me trouble a former executive of the company and wasted HIS time, and what they demanded he do didn’t work either (and went back to him three times). By this point I had spent about 8 hours on the phone with them. Finally I gave up and instructed them to cancel my subscription. They said they would but lo and behold, my credit card showed a charge on the renew date. I called them to cancel again and they said they would. Meanwhile, I had my Visa card block all charges from QB. Next month, another charge – so apparently they managed to force it through. I called QB to cancel, again. Then they said they wouldn’t do this as I was not the Master Admin (although my former exec transferred Admin to me long time ago, more than once.) I said Doesn’t it being my credit card that is being charged give me the right to cancel? No, they said. So now they want me to submit a Change of Master Admin form and then, guess what, call in to cancel again! And spend another 2 hrs with them? Well, I got the Change of Master Admin form and now they want me to upload a bunch of documents to PROVE I am with my company. And guess how I should start to do that? LOGIN! Which is what got me to this whole mess in the first place! 
	Update: They won’t even let my master admin cancel now. They want him to consult our accountant.
	Bottom line: QUICKBOOKS IS TERRIBLE. TOTALLY INCOMPETENT AND UNETHICAL. There are other better accounting software out there, like Zoho Books. You can do much better without wasting your time and dealing with a bunch of crooks.

use_case:
Quickbooks Online is an excellent solution. Their uptime has been exceptional, and their ease-of-use is possibly unmatched. However, definitely check competitors such as Xero to see if they meet your needs at a lower price point and include all of the features that you could possibly want. On this end, although the pricing is expensive, their inclusion of mobile apps makes it a difficult solution to move on from.

I highly recommend this company, but definitely encourage anyone that is not coming from another solution to compare trials from other services to make 100% certain that Quickbooks Online is worth the cost for your organization.

likes: <v ->Hi, my name is Maheen.</v> I am a co-founder and CEO in the mobile app industry. I give QuickBooks a rating of eight out of 10. QuickBooks is a cloud based financial reporting tool. We use it here at our company, and it's a solid performer when it comes down to providing a key financial health matrix. It has great dashboard views, which saves you time both for financial and non-financial managers. It has ample reporting tools, and invoicing, sales, profit-loss statements, so all of your statement needs, financial statement needs are clearly met with this tool. What I really like about QuickBooks online is the fact that you can use the dashboard. It becomes a converged view of all of your financial performance. You can apply rules so that non-financial managers can look at the data that pertains to them. Also, there's a feature called QuickBooks GoPayments that allows you to receive payments from customers on the go so you can actually take your business wherever you are going. Also the fact that there is rules you can set on different levels that basically allows you to safeguard your transactions, your bank transactions, and QuickBooks will flag a transaction as unacceptable if those conditions are met. So it saves you a lot of time, and you don't have to get into the nitty gritty of every transaction. Although QuickBooks is a real solid performer, I really hope and wonder if they can improve the way the physical cash receipts, and the physical receipts from vendors and bills can be ingested in the tool. Sometimes you run into problems where you either have to reload the receipts, take a picture and upload it. So a better way would be QuickBooks to automatically scan your emails for any attachments of receipts. The other consideration is, especially for small to medium size businesses is they really need to allocate a budget for the IT, for the future, for the future needs as there are certain features within QuickBooks which are not free, or you have to pay an add-on fee for that, so the cost of subscription can quickly snowball. It's something for the IT managers to consider when creating a budget. My recommendation for businesses who are considering QuickBooks are basically the size of your staff, of your financial team, how savvy they are with using the cloud based service, and how well-trained they are in a fully automated financial tool. The other thing, of course, is the budgeting part. So the cost has to be within the budget allocated. There are features which suddenly becomes more critical as the business grows, so keep those in mind.

likes: Quickbooks Online allows seamless integration with many other apps and accounts. This enables your Quickbooks application to automatically download transactions from bank accounts, credit cards, payroll providers, expense reporting apps, equity management apps, tax reporting apps, and various inventory management apps. This essentially eliminates the tireless amount of data entry hours and improves the accuracy of entering any transaction. Rules can be set up around recurring transactions so that Quickbooks online intuitively knows how to record each transaction.
dislikes: Some of the reporting features are limited as you are not allowed to build custom reports as easily as in the desktop version.
recommendations: With real time uploads of transaction, our company is able to reconcile on a daily basis. When we receive a month-end statement the majority of transactions have already been confirmed. The month-end closing process has shortened considerably as we are able to keep financial transactions current on a daily basis. Invoicing is another area that has improved dramatically with Quickbooks online. Invoices can be scheduled to go out automatically from the application and emailed directly to customers with options to pay through ACH direct or Quickbooks merchant services. Improved streams of communication with the customer and timeliness of receiving payments have reduced collection cycles and outstanding receivable balances. Accounting

likes: <v ->Hi, my name is Breann.</v> I'm a self-employed bookkeeper in the Southern California region and I give QuickBooks Accountant a 10 out of 10. QuickBooks Accountant Online keeps all my clients in one place making them easily accessible and easier to service everyone. I like that QuickBooks Online Accountant provides you the opportunity to grow your practice and better you for your clients through their pro-advisor training and the incentives that come through that such as better software to use and a better rating amongst potential clients. One aspect of QuickBooks Online Accountant that I believe could use some improving is the process in which you have a new client sign-up for QuickBooks Online. Currently, the process is a little choppy and clients are waiting for email verifications to be sent. Sometimes they're sent, sometimes they're not. Sometimes the login works, sometime they don't. So, I think that making that process a little bit smoother, especially for the accountant that is helping the client sign in, would definitely help improve the software. My recommendation for you if you're thinking about trying QuickBooks Online Accountant is to definitely utilize their support system. They have online chat, where they will not only chat with you online, but if you give them permission they will access your computer and see what you're looking at, what the problem is that you're facing and help walk you through it. They also have a great phone support system and they also have a community where other bookkeepers or accountants have posted their problems and other people have answered according to their experience with the software. So, I wish I would have know that when I first started then

likes: QuickBooks Online has recently improved their offerings to match up more closely with the desktop edition. Now with inventory management in addition to payroll, taxes, and more basic accounting functionality like invoicing, sales receipts, expense reporting, etc., it's hard to imagine a use case for a small business that QuickBooks Online couldn't handle. At a $60 price point and accessible by logging in anywhere, it's also incredibly accessible and convenient.
dislikes: At times QuickBooks Online can be slow to load, and some of the features are a bit clunky. There are quick links for invoices, but to get to estimates you have to move through several steps; the sales tax tracking and reporting system also leaves something to be desired.
recommendations: I use QuickBooks for every accounting task in my small business. The click-through to pay invoices is great, I find the inventory tracking to be invaluable, as well as more standard functions. I like the ability to make a sales receipt as opposed to an invoice for certain purchase categories. Quickbooks
use_case: If you need something more in-depth than Freshbooks or Xero can provide, but want to stay online, give QB online a try!

likes:
As a small business owner, I like that Quickbooks is able to quickly pull in information from my bank statements and intelligently classify transactions once I teach it. The familiarity of using an Intuit product to house all of my accounting data is comforting. Though I haven't yet used it to store sales data, invoices, and credit card data, it is nice that it is there as an option.
Overall, it is a very simple solution to use and, if this speaks to the quality of the software, I have not even once needed to reach out to support.
dislikes:
Sometimes, the online version can have its quirks and the mobile app is not the best user experience. It is more expensive than other options that are available out there. Wave accounting is free, for example. Xero may also be able to offer a less expensive alternative.
Intuit tends to be moderately expensive on offerings like Payroll, Payments, and the like so it gets annoying when these options are promoted throughout the app on occasion.
recommendations: I use this for my accounting and to generate reports to hand over to my accountant come tax season.
use_case: Look into all of your options and don't feel married to the first option you choose. Also, if you are a small business owner who is not too familiar with finance, take some time to learn. It will be well worth it.

likes: The software has a very easy to use interface, very intuitive. I specially like the downloadable desktop app which allows for multiple companies and/or multiple screens for the same company to be open at the same time. Also it is very easy to download G/L information and financial reports to Excel, which is a must for modern day accounting and finance needs.
dislikes: The chart of accounts and financials layout/format is not very flexible. It is ok for a small company with regular accounting classifications, but for a more complex accounting or special categorization, it makes it very difficult to create customized reports. Also, the 1099 functionality is not flexible enough to tag individual transactions for 1099 reporting which makes it difficult to track and forces to create additional accounts to track taxable transactions.
recommendations: Because it is a very easy to use app, it allows to create quick reports, export the information and work with the data to tackle financial reporting needs and audit requests. Additionally, it is easy to clean up past transactions and journal entries and it keeps an audit trail which has proven to be helpful in order to determine the reason or source of certain changes and correct those issues immediately.
use_case: Quickbooks online is a great product for a small business or start up; it is easy to set up, easy to maintain and easy to use. Although the reporting is basic, there are a lot of apps that can be integrated in order to automate consolidation, budgeting, forecasting and financial analysis. However, if the business includes inventory management, then I would definitely recommend another solution as in my opinion QB online does not offer a good solution to manage inventory and cost accounting.

likes: Hi everyone, my name is Bindu, and I'm the CEO Founder of a marketing ad agency. I would give QuickBooks a nine point nine out of 10. So, I use QuickBooks for two specific reasons. One is to reconcile my expenses and my income, and to categorize them for my taxation purposes. And the other is to send out invoices to my clients at the end of every month, or at the end of every project. So, what I love about QuickBooks is really its ability for me to categorize my income, and my expenses, and to be prepared for tax season. And it really is helpful as a small business owner, to know where I am in terms of profits, and like I said, to help with my taxation. So, the only issue with QuickBooks is its learning curve. So, QuickBooks is a little hard, in terms of your initial setup, in terms of figuring out where you go to update your bank statements, to send your invoices. But once you pass that learning curve, things get better from there. So, I do recommend QuickBooks to anyone who's starting their own business, or small business owner. It is great when you want to initially download everything from your business account, business banking account, and reconcile income and expenses as well. As well as work with your invoices. So, it's something

likes:
Quickbooks online has been making some improvements over the past couple of years. I like its cloud-based infrastructure that we can share with our accountants. 

I like that I can use 3rd party apps to upload records.
dislikes:
There is significant latency with the interface, so you have to be very patient while things load. 

While the interface is pretty, I can't decide whether it is unnecessarily complicated, or unnecessarily dumbed down... all I know is that using the software adds to my overall levels of stress and frustration. 

Sometimes, lists of customers or vendors get hidden, so you can't click from customer to customer or vendor to vendor; rather you need to reload the Customer's home tab... there are lots of places like this where you need to click three places to accomplish something that could easily be done with a single click. Want to views the customer on the invoice you're viewing? Remember their name, then cancel out of the Invoice, then open up the customers tab, and then search for her or him. Preposterous. 

I get the sense that Intuit thinks its customers are stupid, so they provide lots of skewomorphic page effects that are completely unnecessary. Open an invoice, and (after 5-15 seconds of loading) it slides in, like a piece of paper, over what you were viewing. I'd rather be able to click intuitively through the system and have the interface open quickly.
recommendations: Collaboration with our off-site accountant.
use_case: Make some tea between clicks.

likes: It is cloud based which helps when on the go. And the tax support is there when you need it. It comes with many currencies for world wide use. Invoices are easily customized. It is easy to learn, far better than QuickBooks Pro software. It is able to organize weekly time sheets. Able to track bank deposits. Another neat feature is that it allows the user to take a picture of bills. Keeping up with any and all proofs of expenses.
dislikes: It's a bit difficult to navigate through the system. Also, it's expensive to have. Customer service is a hassle and dissappointing. A big issue, if not done right the first time, is that the company cannot be reverted. Cannot change the description of a category after several attempts. It can not automatically add any late penalties causing you to use an outside software service.
recommendations: None at the moment. I need more time using this software.

likes: When Quickbooks Online works, it is fantastic. However, you are all but guaranteed to have some issue, small or large, that will require you to speak with someone in support. Generally the support staff has tried to be helpful, but for most issues for which you'd need to call they have no way to help you.
dislikes:
Som examples of problems I've had are:
-Company files being corrupted at random times
-Bank and credit card accounts not syncing correctly
-Company files not uploading correctly from QB desktop
-Subscription billing payments not linking correctly to the account (so the credit card continued to be charged on automatic billing, but Intuit shut down the account-people in billing could not find the charges despite bank reference numbers and Intuit's support phone number being listed with the charge). Tremendous pain to have to cancel the account entirely, start over, contest credit card charges, etc.
-Incorrect payroll calculations
-The list goes on and on
recommendations: East reporting.
use_case: It's hard to say whether Intuit will work out the bugs ever. If they did, QBO would be a great product. As it is, any small issues turns into many hours, possibly days of work to correct Intuit's problems.

bonus_data.title: Atrocious support with unbelievably long wait times

likes: QBO has a lot of features that I don't need (yet?), so it makes me feel that this software will be able to grow with my business.
dislikes:
- I wish that QBO allowed the user to take personal backups of the books. I want to be able to revert back to a previous version in case something gets messed up during a bookkeeping session.
- I hate that there's not an upgrade path from "Self-Employed" to "Small Business" versions of QBO. Intuit tries to force self-employed users into buying more than they need from the beginning. For those who go with the cheaper "Self-Employed" version, there is no way to migrate your books to a "Small Business" version to QBO. You'll either have to maintain two accounts or manually re-enter everything from Self-Employed into Small Business.
recommendations: QBO allows us to keep a record of our transactions, plain and simple.
use_case: QBO certainly isn't perfect, but it's the market leader and if you work with an accounting firm, this is the software that they are likely to be familiar with (or the desktop version of QuickBooks). QBO is here to stay, so I recommend getting aboard and learning it. Avoid signing up for QBO "Self-Employed" until Intuit creates a migration path between "Self-Employed" and "Small Business" so that you don't have the headache of having to manually re-enter all your books.

likes: <v ->Hi, my name is Jennifer.</v> I'm the director of a music school and I give QuickBooks a nine out of 10. I use QuickBooks to invoice my clients, keep track of how much they owe on their accounts and also how much they've paid, and also to print checks for my employees and any other vendors that I need to pay. My favorite thing about QuickBooks is how user-friendly it is. It's really easy to navigate the browser and I can generally find everything I need. They also have really good customer service. I am able to get on a chat window at any time with any questions I may have, or I can search through their help database and find answers that I need. I also like that they're always updating, so when you subscribe to the online service they're constantly adding new features or making things even better than they already are. There's not really that much that I dislike about QuickBooks. I think it would be neat to have a better feature for keeping track of all transactions for a client. For example, my clients are billed for a whole semester and make payments on it, and sometimes it's a little hard to keep track of all that just in their individual invoices. Also, if you don't have the online version and you download the actual software they don't offer any support for that, which I find a little weird and discouraging. But other than that, I like everything. If you're thinking about trying QuickBooks, I'd recommend first deciding what your actual needs are for your business. QuickBooks offers several different packages for the online version, and I use the Simple Start version. It's the cheapest and most simple. And then you can add features from there or upgrade to a more detailed package. So just think about what you need. You can use something like PayPal and you can do invoicing on there. I would say that would be better if you have just a few clients, but if you have many clients that you need to invoice and also do other features, QuickBooks is really great for that.

bonus_data.title: So just think about what your specific needs are before getting it, and you can always add on to it

likes: I love the features. It's very comprehensive and organized and the UI is very friendly. I think there's a lot of features that I have yet to explore, but the ones I use more than serve my needs in terms of keeping IRS compliance. Customer service is also really great and quick. I can almost always get a member of the Service team within a day or two. The onboarding process was also really quick. My favorite part is that it is online, which serves the needs of my online-team
dislikes: There's too many features that I pay for but don't use. And that is discouraging as a customer. I wish there was a scaled down version at a lower price.
recommendations: Financial tracking and computing financial statements for taxes online. My team is all online run, so we needed an online solution.
use_case: If you want an online accounting solution that has the brand name, is reliable and does it's work at a reasonable price, buy QuickBooks Online. I tried it for my firm and it was very easy to use. My firm is always online-run and small, so our transaction history can be a bit all over the place. QuickBooks has helped us keep everything together and produce our financial statements when needed.

likes: The only good thing about Quickbooks Online is that links automatically to your bank, but then again, that feature is not unique. For anyone considering moving from Quickbooks Desktop to Quickbooks Online, consider that the process will not be simple, and once you move your data, Quickbooks may change pricing policies at a moment's notice. The functionality is also more restrictive. It is simply a poorly designed product and the support staff often struggle to understand why it does not work.
dislikes: I recently decided to convert from an old version of Quickbooks desktop to Quickbooks online. The experience was horrible. I have spent probably 20 hours on the phone with their support staff because things simply dont work on their platform. First, the conversion of data would simply not work. It took 4 different support people and hours of attempts to finally have them shut the account and start over from scratch. No reason it would not work, just a glitch. Second, there have been multiple things that just did not work. The connection to Wells Fargo started inputting deposits as expenses, and I had to manually correct them. The state tax deposits would not work electronically, so I once again had to get support to fix it. And then the ultimate insult is that they changed their pricing policy and doubled the previously discounted price after one month. Their pricing policy seems to be bait and switch. I am also acting as an accountant for a second company, and have advised them to not switch to Quickbooks for payroll. I'm pretty sure I will be finding another solution and cancelling this Quickbooks subscription in the next week.
recommendations: I wanted a solution that would quickly and easily pull in data from banks and process payroll and provide basic accounting features, with enough flexibility to allow me to manually adjust entries easily. But there is nothing easy or powerful about Quickbooks Online. It is the perfect combination of glitchy and restrictive. Constant Problems and Then
use_case: Be careful of what appear to be good offers, they often disappear.

reviewer_name: Verified User in Health, Wellness and Fitness

likes: I like the ease of syncing bank accounts and creating reports. It also populates information in an easy to "view" format. I love the report feature as it makes it very easy to do different reports and to customize by date or other features. It's then easy to download in several different file formats to send to other people in the company. The upside is it's easy to monitor spending and expenses so we know where to cut back or make changes. In our field, summer months are very busy and winter is slow. We know where to cut back during winter and also how to view this all in a longer range format.
dislikes: It can be counter-intuitive at points, it's difficult to delete things if you need to redo something, and I need to use a third-party software to import data for sales. I also have had to use a third-party software in order to do mass deleting when I accidentally uploaded files to the wrong area.
recommendations: It makes it easier for me to do end of month profit and loss statements and to quickly check accounts for deposits. The benefits we've realized is that you can compare month to month profit and loss, so we can compare months. This allows us to see where we've spent more or paid employees more or less so we can accurately monitor the process and loss.
use_case: If you're already experience with accounting software, this may be a good fit, but I feel like there are better options.

bonus_data.title: Very easy to use and manage

likes:
It is a leading program and that is respected, so many people can not be wrong.

Being a leading product, the information that is available is enough, a lot of user experience, many guides, a lot of information on the web, there is always someone to ask, for which there is a lot of information about the program, which clarify the panorama.

The implementation and the learning curve is not at all complicated, there is a lot of available personnel that manages the tool.

Being basically an accounting tool, you can cohabit with CRM or ERP already existing in the company.

The levels of integration and adaptation with other tools is adequate and not at all cumbersome at those levels.

Talking about a tool with the surname online, means speed, accessibility, connectivity, storage, freedom, automatic updates, without physical spaces or schedules that limit the activity, infinite possibilities of income in terms of devices. In short a world of perfectible options every day.

Regarding the tool as such we can highlight the module of banks and reports, it is very complete, with automatic updates, complete bank reconciliation, inclusion of bank accounts in a simple manner, reports, migration to the company's accounting directly.

The payroll module and the possibility of tax calculations.

One of the aspects that I like the most is the possibility of managing estimates before generating final invoices, which allows negotiation margin with clients and competitive adjustments.

The desktop is friendly, visually striking, allows you to create icons of direct access to the most important options.

The management of scalability levels is simple and secure.

The basic reports are included in the essential version.

Unlike other programs of this style, the mobile application is very well developed and integrated.
dislikes:
In the points of improvements we can highlight the following:

The relation cost benefits, for the companies that look for an ERP or a CRM to invest in this program guarantees the countable part, of the rest they must develop the planning of the business.

I can not believe that the option of the inventory module is not included in the basic version, perhaps the handling of inventory is not part of the day to day of any business?

All the information, courses, guides and the program itself, does not offer the option in Spanish. Therefore, the use of English is essential at all levels of application of this program.

Being a tool developed in the cloud, the requirements at the hardware level increase in comparison with other tools.

the integration with other environments and the handling of the electronic mail can improve.
recommendations: Undoubtedly the management of bank accounts, reconciliations, the listing of bank listings, had always been a problem. And not to mention the moment of including all these reconciliations in the final accounting of the company. Nowadays this is not a headache. This tool is widely recommended to handle these situations.
use_case:
In summary we can determine two scenarios:

In the first, if the focus of the need for your business points to the accounting part, how much you enter, how much is earned, the management of banks, payroll and taxes ... QBO is the tool, because you will have all this added to the benefits of an online tool with development in the cloud.

In the second scenario, if the focus of your business is to acquire a CRM or an ERP, integrate the planning of all areas of your business, managing the client portfolio, strengthening the sales force and the production area, as well like, having a clear workflow ... Then you should study other different options.

bonus_data.title: The accounting of your company with quick access from the cloud

likes: Don't like it at all. Not user friendly after being used to using desktop version. Can't find anything.
dislikes: Not user friendly. Menus not the same as desktop version. Can't print reports I need.
use_case: None

likes: The best thing about QuickBooks to me is how easily it integrates with my bank accounts, credit cards, and payroll service. Having everything update in one place automatically is so much easier than doing things manually, which is exactly what I used to do. I am also impressed by the fact that the more you use it, the more it learns your business. This means that after a short "training" period it becomes, for the most part, very fast to use.
dislikes: So far, the only thing I've found that I don't like about QuickBooks Online is when I run into an unresolved error or problem that I'm not sure how to solve. Sometimes I feel that it takes a little to long to find the answer to a problem or know where to turn for support. With that said, I do remember one instance in which I had a problem and I ended up on the phone with a QuickBooks customer service agent for a very long time as they helped me figure things out. Also, and this is a very minor gripe, but it bugs me that every time I go to the website and click "log in" it has to open a new tab...I already subscribed; stop selling me.
recommendations: I run a small business with just a few full time employees. I used to do all my bookkeeping with spreadsheets that I made from scratch. They worked fine, but everything was completely manual and if I got behind in keeping track of my expenses -- which I always did -- I then had to sit for hours and hours and get everything back on track. At the end of the year I had to clear 2-3 days just to prep for paying my taxes. With QuickBooks everything is always up to date and my accountant is finally off my back about using it instead of my own system. Why didn't
use_case: When I started with QuickBooks Online I was completely new to any type of accounting software other than my own manual bookkeeping done with spreadsheets. To get started, I worked with a local accounting firm that specialized in Quickbooks and provided hands-on training. I found it extremely helpful to do one-on-one training with a consultant. A couple of days of training got me up and running and totally comfortable in no time.

likes: The ability to access my accounts from anywhere with WiFi and the user friendly interface.
dislikes: The reports and overall customization is extremely limited; also the cost is pretty expensive compared to the desktop version.

likes: It's online, so access is simple and can be done from anywhere. It can be localized for the country you are working in. I use it to do the books for my UK subsidiary.
dislikes: You can only have a single window open at a given time. This makes it difficult to view two reports for comparison. Also, it refreshes a report automatically each time you change a single date. This is annoying when you are trying to do a month date range.
recommendations: Again, it is online, so my UK audit firm can access it to do our audit and tax returns. It is localized so all of the necessary UK items are there.
use_case: This software is designed for someone who is NOT an accountant. It is best for a small business owner who has little to no knowledge of accounting. If you like the way this software works, but are an accountant, you would be better served by Peachtree accounting software.
reviewer_name: Verified User in Computer & Network Security

likes: When you email and invoice, but then need to make a slight change, it automatically updates as long as the customer clicks on the invoice in the email.
dislikes: The customer must actually click on the invoice in an email in order to see any updates including that it was paid. Most customers fail to click on it so we tend to send a lot of secondary emails. Also, when customers pay online from their bank account, sometimes it does not accurately show up in the payments so we have to check every deposit versus trusting the system.
recommendations: I used to use excel to create invoices, which worked well, but then I had to use a separate file to keep track of payments. Quickbooks brought it all together and also tracks payroll and my bank account so I rarely have toe manually enter anything!
use_case: Look into online payments and credit card fees before committing.

reviewer_name: Verified User in Leisure, Travel & Tourism

likes: No-fuss online access for multiple users.
dislikes:
Online access for multiple users is easy to set up, but the program is often slow to respond, even over a good internet connection.

Lacks the Advanced Search of all transactions, regardless of type, that makes QB Desktop so much more flexible and easier to use.

QBO also hides advanced options (delete transaction, undo reconciliation, reverse transaction) behind multiple layers of menus. I understand this to be part of an effort to make the product friendlier to non-bookkeepers, but I use the Accountant version, and these functions are still hidden under layers of GUI. I far prefer the capacity that QB Desktop offers to strip the GUI down to a workspace and a full selection of drop-down menus.

Lacks a real inventory system. For that, you will have to use an outside app, and that is going to raise the cost.

It is also near impossible to create your own solution for missing features, as Intuit has obscured the underlying database mark-up language. For instance, if you are a non-profit that need to apply customer and class tags to multiple transaction lines for later reporting, it is far quicker and easier to do that in a spreadsheet and import the transactions. But you can only DIY that in QuickBooks Desktop and with its publicly accessible IIF mark-up language. If you use QBO, good luck finding (and paying for) a third-party app to fill this need.

I also find that the labeling of transactions (e.g., Expenditure is more prominent than Bill) and the prominence with which the bank integration/transaction matching feature is promoted to conceptually favor cash accounting practices, which are not preferred or even legal for most businesses. But they're intuitive to casual users, and the so the interface leads casual users down garden paths that an outside bookkeeper will eventually have to fix before tax (or worse, audit) time. I've earned plenty of money fixing the problems that QBO leads casual users into, but I wish it didn't encourage them into those problems in the first place.

I also find the GUI distracting and obscuring in its own right. So much screen real estate is spent, even in the Ultra Compact display mode, on graphics that are supposed to be user friendly that I can never see as much actual financial information as I would like on one screen.

Also, the bulk-editing functions in the accountant version of QBO are hobbled compared to those in QB Desktop.
recommendations: Accounting software with no-fuss online access for multiple users.
use_case:
If you really must use QBO, do not use it without guidance if you do not have a solid working knowledge of accrual accounting; hire someone who does to do your bookkeeping instead.

If you have bookkeeping needs that are at all complex, I strongly encourage you to consider hosting QB Desktop on an application server instead. If you aren't sure how complex your needs are, consult an experienced bookkeeper before you choose your platform.

likes: Back in the beginning, QB was deemed an "Accountant's Nightmare and a Bookkeeper's Dream". After 28 years of working with QB and as a bookkeeper and an accountant and an owner, I feel I can speak from all sides. QBO is a wonderful tool for owners who are constantly on the move. Everything is linked, you can get up to the minute information about the status of your financial information and so much more. However, without getting into details, a bookkeeper's job is data entry. Depending on the level of the bookkeeper determines the type of work of course. Regardless of the tasks, I have only seen tasks that would take 15 minutes on a desktop version can take 2 to 3 times as long with the online version and this is if there are no issues with online connectivity. Intuit clearly needs to develop a hybrid system where a bookkeeper can work locally on a PC without any worries of technical issues with a link that hooks to the online version for the owner to see what is going on. I have seen so much waste over the years and honestly do not understand this new generation where waste is an acceptable cost of doing business.
dislikes: Bank feed is ridiculous for a bookkeeper. A bookkeeper should have already entered everything before it hits the bank. What I see happening all the time is the owner Adding or Transferring instead of simply ignoring this feed causing the bookkeeper to have to correct the errors.
use_case: Added expense to your accounting budget. What should be capable of being done by one or two takes three or four and sometimes more people to accomplish tasks.

switched_from.reason: Owners demanded online connectivity. There is no comparison. They are two totally different worlds.

use_case: We love QBO for the overall ease of use. We can tackle invoicing easily and the reporting customization is excellent for our needs.

likes: In my workplace we collect a lot of payments and being able to track not only the finances but also our inventory is crucial. I like how you can just search up a customer and create a sales receipt in no time. The memo box also allows me to add a ticket number to keep track of every specific ticket and also scan my receipts and upload them attached to the online receipt!
dislikes: I always use the “payment by customer” file and it would be helpful if it was always automatically there so I didn’t have to type it in everytime. Aside from that, the site works wonderfully and definitely make the day to day operations run much more smoothly.
recommendations: QuickBooks puts all of the businesses receipts in one place and allows us to know where the money is going and make sure that we keep track of every payment collected.
use_case: QuickBooks is the best and fastest way to put all your payments in one place and manage your budgeting.
